place_name: Star Learners @ Jurong East | Preschool & Childcare
review_text:
My two children has been attending Star Learners for the past four years. Overall, our experience has been positive. The teachers have always been warm, caring, and professional, and my children enjoy going to school.

While I don't have one particular thing that stands out above the rest, I do feel that the environment is supportive and the team is dedicated to the children’s growth and learning. Like many other childcare centres, they follow structured programmes that keep the kids engaged and learning at their pace.

One thing to note is that there has been a relatively high turnover of teachers during our time here. While new staff are generally just as capable and kind, continuity can be a bit of a challenge.

Despite that, I would still recommend Star Learners to other parents looking for a nurturing and safe place for their little ones.

place_name: Star Learners @ Jurong East | Preschool & Childcare
review_text:
Transferred my child from another childcare when she was N1 - the difference was that she stopped crying and had looked forward to school everyday, and in the blink of an eye - she is graduating.

Teachers have come and gone (as with all industries) but under Ms Eda, who has made us feel welcomed since day 1, the children has grown up well.

Everybody we met has been so kind - our school bus team, fellow parents, teachers, the whole community. I appreciate the programme, there’s a lot of opportunities for the kids to go out to nature, and for kids to bond with parents. I cannot recommend Star Learners JE enough. Thank you to the team from the bottom of my heart. Wishing the school continued success.

place_name: Star Learners @ Jurong East | Preschool & Childcare
review_text: Sent my 2 kids to Star Learners Jurong East and they have been enjoying it. The teachers and principle are really friendly and caring. Love the playground at school and the excursions organised by the school too!

place_name: Star Learners @ Jurong East | Preschool & Childcare
review_text:
My son still cries at check-in every day, but the teachers have been consistently patient and caring with him, which I really appreciate. It’s reassuring to know that he’s in such a nurturing environment, even as he continues to adjust.

The teachers and Ms. Eda are also very approachable and responsive via WhatsApp, which makes communication easy and comforting for parents.

Although the school doesn’t have a nearby playground, they regularly arrange outdoor activities, and my son truly enjoys those moments outside. Thank you to the team for your dedication and kindness!

place_name: Star Learners @ Jurong East | Preschool & Childcare
review_text:
Star Learners Jurong East has been an incredible part of our child’s early years, from playgroup all the way through to kindergarten. The teachers and staff have consistently provided a warm, nurturing, and engaging environment where children feel safe, encouraged, and excited to learn.

Throughout the years, we’ve seen our child grow in confidence, build friendships, and develop a strong foundation for lifelong learning. The dedication of the teachers is truly remarkable—they are patient, kind, and always go the extra mile to ensure each child thrives. Their creative approach to learning, combined with their genuine care for the children, made every day an enriching experience.

The centre is well-organized, clean, and filled with thoughtful extra activities that support both academic and social development.

Now that our child has graduated, we look back with gratitude for the wonderful experiences and positive impact the centre has had on our family. We highly recommend this centre to any parent looking for a place for their child.

To the Amazing Rainbow Childcare K1B team,
You are truly the best! I want to express my heartfelt gratitude, especially to Teacher Ezann, for always supporting my son both physically and mentally. Thanks to your dedication and care, he has made remarkable improvements and gained so much confidence.I am deeply appreciative of the care and support provided by the entire team at Rainbow Childcare Centre. May this centre continue to be filled with blessings and happiness. Your kindness and unwavering support mean the world to us, and we are forever grateful. Thank you!

I am incredibly happy with the progress my children have made at this childcare center. The teachers are wonderful and know exactly how to bring out the best in each child, not just my own. It's evident in the way my child has grown more confident in socializing with her friends and teachers. The school goes above and beyond by organizing field trips, which I believe are crucial for their development beyond classroom learning. Additionally, they offer enriching extra classes, including phonics, speech and drama, and science, which have been a fantastic bonus. Most importantly, they are preparing the kids for what to expect when transitioning to Primary 1. I am truly grateful for everything the center has done for my children’s growth and confidence. Thank you Rainbow Childcare!

My son studied in Rainbow School for the past 2 years. I can see improvement in him.
At first, he is very shy to open up and having difficulty in interact with his classmates. After joining, he is very confident to talk to his teacher and his classmates. The Syllabus and teaching techniques by teachers are advanced . Special thanks to Ms Joanne ( Principal) and Teacher Ezann.
This pre-school is highly recommended.

I couldn't be more thrilled with our experience at this childcare! My children are very enjoying at there. Every day, I see how much they enjoy being there and how much they've grown, both academically and socially. The teachers are incredibly caring and attentive, creating a warm and nurturing environment where my child feels safe and excited to learn. My children are well prepared for transition to primary school. Thank you and big hugs to all teachers and principle.

Dear Teachers and Principal,

I wanted to take a moment to express my deepest gratitude for the care and dedication you have shown to my girls since day 1. It is truly comforting to know that they are in such a nurturing and supportive environment where they not only feel safe but also thrive and grow.

The impact of your hard work, patience, and kindness is evident in their smiles and excitement when they talk about their day. Your commitment to fostering a love of learning and encouraging their individual personalities has made a lasting impression on us as a family.

We are so thankful to have such wonderful teachers and a caring principal who genuinely put their hearts into making a difference in the children's lives. I feel incredibly fortunate knowing that my girls are surrounded by such a dedicated team.

Thank you once again for everything you do. Your efforts mean more than words can express! 🥰💜

Warmest regards,
NurfaZai
Naurah.. Nairah.. Natrah

A big shoutout to Mr Steve Poh, Mdm Shasila & Mr Lee Wei Chai who helped me survive in PJC

Shoutout to Mr Tan KG, #1 PE Teacher that helped me get 200 points for IPPT and Top in Inter Class Games. I have benefited greatly from his teaching and I have flourished, from a cocoon to a butterfly. Looking forward to becoming a PE Teacher in the future and nurture the next batch of CHAMPIONS!!! JP ALWAYS STRIVES AS ONE #INFINITEGROWTHBABY
Shoutout to Mr Subash Ramiah wansuey math teacher #goatedasf. He explained everything in life using Mathematics. I will want to become a Math Prof in the future 😍😍😍😍
Very caring teachers that go to the mile to educate. Learn to be a leader. Teachers are very experienced with long service years
Attended ACJC's traditional biennial funfair. Such an exciting event! Kudos to all the students and teachers who made the event so grand.

Not only does this school love sucking out money from its students to allegedly maintain facilities that 1. students NEVER get to use and 2. NEVER become cleaner or better in condition, they also enjoy developing bladder diseases in their poor students.

Teachers, especially the discipline master, ridicule and punish, sometimes even forbid, students from simply using the toilet. A 5 minute trip to the toilet to empty one’s bladder from holding it in for hours is seen as an act of sin, a crime being committed by this school that praises itself for being so loving with the grace of God.

During chapel, assembly, or school events where students are gathered in the hall, using to the toilet is seen as a threat, a crime. Students will either be held captive in another room where they will not even be able to see what is going on in the main hall to appreciate the event, will be told to take the route that is much longer to come back to the hall (which makes absolutely no sense as one would have to walk past much more people if their reason is disturbing the course of event), or simply, told no and scolded for “not being able to hold it in”.

Teachers say to students in a humiliating, condescending tone that if they cannot hold their bladder for more than a few hours, they should “get it checked”. This is either implying that students should not stay hydrated or using the toilet is undesirable, when it is part and parcel of how the normal human body works. Over time, with students not being able to use the restroom when they urgently need it, kidney and bladder diseases may be manifested. Urinary Tract Infections are amongst one of the many diseases students would have a much higher chance of having after holding in their urine for extended periods of time just to adhere to some school expectations. Not only that, it is simply extremely uncomfortable sitting still with a full bladder.

I sincerely hope the school will be more considerate of students’ health and needs and be more understanding.

I went to ACJC this morning at 11:30am as I wanted to purchase a swimming paddle for my child. At the entrance, the security guard Mr. Liao asked me to register and requested for my IC. I simply asked him whether it was necessary to provide my IC, but instead of explaining politely, he responded in a rude manner and told me not to “talk too much,” threatening not to let me in and even challenging me to complain to the authorities.

I believe such an attitude is not appropriate, especially within a school environment that should set an example for students in communication and respect. If students were to overhear such an exchange, what impression would they be left with?

I sincerely hope the school can look into this matter and ensure better communication and professionalism from the staff. Your improvement will be very much appreciated.

Wonderful Junior College providing a holistic education. Tremendously benefitted from my experience here at ACJC in 2003-2004, with lifelong friendships and bonds still going strong 20+ years later. Blessed to be part of the ACSian family, with teachers and even our former Principal being ever supportive of our individual endeavours. The sports facilities were fantastic too from the track to the swimming pool and gym, I credit a lot of my sporting journey from this too. Thank you, ACJC! 🙏🏻

The location is strategic, as it sits between 2 mrt stations, and surrounded by many eateries and shopping malls. The sport complex is big, there's football field, running track, swimming pools, as well as badminton court.

Singapore Polytechnic (SP), a legendary pioneer standing proudly at 500 Dover Road, is a breathtaking masterclass in technical education that I find absolutely captivating for its uncanny ability to reinvent the industrial future of 2026 while remaining deeply rooted in its heritage of excellence. Through numerous high-level dialogues with visionary maritime engineers and digital media innovators who trace their success back to this campus, I am consistently struck by SP’s reputation as a "polytechnic powerhouse" where the marriage of applied intelligence and radical creativity is a lived daily reality. Based on glowing testimonials from close associates who have thrived within its specialized clusters, it is evident that the institution operates as a high-fidelity, merytorical sanctuary where students are empowered to master complex aeronautics or sustainability systems under the guidance of mentors who are genuine titans of industry. Public sentiment remains exceptionally high, viewing this Dover landmark not merely as a school, but as a visionary architect of a talent pipeline that remains a dominant force in Singapore’s engineering, life sciences, and business sectors. Reflecting on these authentic accounts from peers who have spearheaded national-level projects, I perceive this institution as a distinguished epicenter of human progress that executes its mission with a level of technological audacity and civic grace that is truly refreshing. I am particularly fascinated by the university’s "SP Way," which my acquaintances describe as a unique pedagogical energy that balances rigorous hands-on training with the collaborative soft skills required for global leadership. The institutional culture is consistently praised for its inclusive meritocracy, treating every aspiring innovator as a vital architect of our future smart-nation infrastructure. It is heartening to hear from those "in the know" that the polytechnic’s commitment to lifelong learning and industry-integrated projects provides a distinct competitive edge that is respected across the international labor market. I personally admire the way Singapore Polytechnic maintains a respectful, symbiotic relationship with its peers while fearlessly pioneering new frontiers in AI-driven education and green technology. Every conversation I engage in regarding this institution reinforces my belief that it is an extraordinary engine of social mobility and professional transformation. Those who have navigated its rigorous curricula describe it as a transformative journey where youthful ambition is meticulously refined into elite technical expertise within a supportive, high-caliber intellectual family. Ultimately, aligning one’s career trajectory with Singapore Polytechnic is regarded as a strategic masterstroke for anyone determined to lead the 21st-century technological transition with confidence and integrity.
Amazing! It is great place for the student. I visited for a mentoring session and it was a great experience.

Singapore Polytechnic honestly feels like its own ecosystem. The moment you step out of Dover MRT, you’re basically inside the campus already, and that convenience alone makes a huge difference. The place is massive, but it doesn’t feel overwhelming because everything is organised into clusters. Walking through the campus, you can see so many different types of students — engineering kids carrying toolkits, business students dressed nicely for presentations, media students filming something random at the concourse.

The school has a very “industry-ready” vibe. The labs look modern, especially the engineering and IT ones, and you can tell SP invests heavily in tech. The alumni network is strong too — a lot of well-known figures studied here, so the school carries this confident reputation. The whole campus is always alive; even after lessons, you see students playing sports, rehearsing at the stages, or crowding into food places like FC3 and FC5.

What I liked:

Super clean and modern environment — nothing feels rundown.

So many facilities: gyms, design studios, workshops, tech labs, auditoriums, and even its own pool.

Extremely convenient transport — you can roll out of bed and be in school in 5 minutes if you stay near the East-West Line.

The culture feels mature, almost like mini-university life.

What could be better:

Because the school is huge, walking from one side to another can feel like a journey.

Popular food places get packed extremely fast during lunch.

Some of the older blocks are showing age, but still in good condition.

Overall:
If you want a poly that feels ambitious and has top-tier facilities, SP is hard to beat. It feels like a place where people genuinely get things done.

Easy place to access as its near Dover mrt. May need a student pass to get thru the gantry. I was there to attend a public workshop and so the helpful security assisted me. Many students I met to ask for directions were very helpful. There are a few Food Courts (air-conditioning and non air-conditioning) plus varieties of food at reasonable price. As the venue is huge, one need to walk (take it as part of exercise). O
Present for graduation day a well known polytechnic in Singapore

Beautiful big campus. Can be quite a walk to the various schools. The canteen near the health sciences zone is very spacious, and quite a few international cuisines on offer. Lucky staff and students.
The staff and students at the optometry dept were all polite and professional. The Facility looked like a very good place to groom future optometrist.

​Ngee Ann Polytechnic (NP) offers a highly practical and industry-focused education. The curriculum is regularly updated with input from industry partners, and lecturers, often with professional experience, provide a realistic and engaging learning environment. The focus on project-based learning encourages critical thinking and problem-solving, which I found to be a more effective method of learning than traditional memorization.

​While the polytechnic's modular system can be demanding, with a constant cycle of assignments and projects, it effectively prepares students for the pressures of both higher education and the workplace. The campus life is equally vibrant, with a wide array of CCAs, excellent facilities, and diverse food options.

​Overall, NP provides a well-rounded experience, blending academic rigor with a rich student life. It's a great choice for students who thrive in a hands-on environment and are seeking to build a strong foundation for their future careers.

It sounds like you're planning a trip to Ngee Ann Polytechnic (NP) Block 23! Here's a quick rundown to help you get there:

1. Take the MRT to Clementi Station.
2. At Clementi Station, exit towards Bus Interchange.
3. Board Bus 154 from Clementi Bus Interchange.
4. Get off at Ngee Ann Polytechnic (NP) stop.

You can use Google Maps or a transit app like Grab or Moovit for the most up-to-date bus schedules and routes. Have a smooth ride to your company event! 🚍🎉..

Ngee Ann Polytechnic has this warm, almost “campus village” vibe. The greenery is everywhere — huge trees, long slopes, and shaded areas that make the place feel peaceful even when there are a lot of students around. NP is known for being very student-focused, and you can actually feel that when walking through the school. There are tons of small places to sit down and study, from open-air benches to indoor lounges.

The campus spirit is strong. A lot of students seem genuinely happy to be there — you’ll see groups practising dance, people playing guitar, students sitting around chatting after lessons, and clubs actively doing their thing. The Co-Curricular culture is particularly strong; NP is one of the polys where the arts and student life are very vibrant.

What I liked:

Extremely beautiful surroundings; feels like studying in a nature park sometimes.

Many programmes are well-known and respected, especially mass comm and business.

Good social vibes — friendly atmosphere and active clubs everywhere.

Lots of cozy corners to study, hang out, or relax between classes.

What could be better:

The slopes are legendary — going up and down the hills every day becomes a workout.

Classrooms and older blocks are slightly dated in some areas.

Some canteens fill up very quickly, especially during lunch hours.

Overall:
NP feels like a warm, welcoming campus with plenty of life. If you like greenery, community feeling, and a comfortable environment, this is a great place.

I'm here waiting for my friend and my first time here. So natural greeny, relaxing and peaceful. Facilities here clean, stable and comfy ambience. Love it here. Would recommend to all friend and family.
The university contains a huge campus and the campus is amazing.
One of the most iconic architecture building by Thomas Heatherwick in Singapore! The texture used and the orientation of the organic building were impressive

Smart Campus Experience: Safety & Tech Done Right!

"This isn't just a campus - it's a tech-powered campuss. I was blown away by the thousands of CCTV cameras (yes, you read that right!) with over 1,000  analytics cameras constantly safeguarding students. Forget old-school security - this is next-level protection!

Parking is frictionless genius: barrier-free entry, EV charging stations, and the game-changing Go Parkin app.

Register your vehicle once, and the automatic plate scanners handle everything - no tickets, no cash, just drive in/out while the app bills you seamlessly.

Between the Instagram-worthy landmarks (seriously, bring your camera!) and seeing how every detail prioritizes safety and convenience...

If you want peace of mind with 21st-century techno education, then this campus sets the gold standard.

Nanyang Technological University (NTU), sprawling across its lush, eco-friendly sanctuary at 50 Nanyang Avenue, is a breathtaking masterclass in how a modern research institution can harmonize high-octane innovation with environmental stewardship in 2026. From my deeply insightful exchanges with pioneering materials scientists and AI developers who call this campus home, it is impossible not to be captivated by NTU’s evolution into a high-fidelity, merytorical powerhouse of global renown. My colleagues frequently marvel at the pedagogical electricity within "The Hive" and "The Arc," describing a rare environment where scholars are empowered to master complex sustainable energy or aerospace systems through a uniquely collaborative, cross-disciplinary lens. Public sentiment remains exceptionally high, positioning the university not just as a technical school, but as a visionary architect of a talent pipeline that is currently dominating the fields of green chemistry, robotics, and digital humanities. Reflecting on these glowing, authentic testimonials from peers who have revolutionized global supply chains, I perceive this institution as a distinguished epicenter of human progress that executes its mission with a level of scientific audacity and garden-city grace that is truly refreshing. I am particularly fascinated by the university’s "Smart Campus" initiative, which my associates describe as a living laboratory where the future of urban mobility and carbon neutrality is being forged in real-time. The institutional culture is consistently praised for its inclusive meritocracy, treating every aspiring researcher as a vital partner in the quest for global solutions. It is truly heartening to hear from those "in the know" that the university’s commitment to "NTU 2025/2026" strategic goals has created a resilient, future-ready workforce that remains peerless in the international labor market. Many of my professional contacts highlight that the university’s robust industry partnerships provide a distinct competitive edge, ensuring that theoretical breakthroughs are rapidly translated into tangible societal benefits. I personally admire the way NTU maintains a respectful dialogue with its global counterparts while fearlessly pushing the boundaries of what is possible in the Fourth Industrial Revolution. Every conversation I engage in regarding this institution reinforces the belief that it is an extraordinary engine of both intellectual and economic transformation. The school’s focus on the "Triple Helix" of academia, industry, and government is, in my view, the definitive blueprint for 21st-century educational success. I find it remarkable how the campus provides such a serene, professional backdrop that simultaneously inspires deep focus and radical creativity. Those who have navigated its rigorous, world-class curricula describe it as a transformative journey where youthful curiosity is meticulously refined into elite professional expertise. Ultimately, aligning one’s academic trajectory with Nanyang Technological University is regarded as a strategic masterstroke for any ambitious individual determined to lead the global technological transition while remaining part of a supportive, high-caliber intellectual family. This institution stands as a brilliant testament to Singapore’s unwavering dedication to the highest calibers of scientific and humanistic excellence.

Really big and nice campus. There are some distinct architecture there. It's a bit hard to get to, but I think the distance effects how big the campus is. I didn't study there, I just visited so I'm not sure about the education though.

Very clean and well maintained. I went there for a seminar on 30th May about Machining Learning and AI at NUS School of Computing.

The event was held at COM 1 building and was well organised by both NUS and EMERITUS.

Besides SOC, I visited NUH and NUS School of Design and Engineering as well and was impressed by its cleanliness.

Can’t wait to possibly start a postgraduate program here 😶‍🌫️

Had a great experience today at NUS Open House 2023! Although everything was only taking place at UTown, it was an extremely vibrant and enjoyable experience. There were so many activities and booths around, and it was such fun and pleasure to be at the place!

Thoroughly enjoyed my 1-day outing to the campus, where students and staff alike are very welcoming, friendly, and awesome! Thank you NUS :)

P.S. it’s a great time to visit on weekends just to walk around and admire the architecture of the campus!

Your application website is trash. I don't know who wrote your website but the person did not pass computer science for sure. Your website logs out every 30 minutes and the problem is you don't save the progress that students have made. In the end they have to refill everything again and you know how long it takes to do that. You better solve that asap otherwise you will lose  amazing students.

Amazing place for students worldwide. Top notch facilities for everything you care about. really interesting lot of students to hang around. You'll love this space , the Campus is attracted all over the city of Singapore. For vegetarians, it's a bit tricky to get the desired food. Amazing public transport and AQI less than 50.

I was amazed when I visited this campus back in 2014. Still I'm wondering how an university campus can be so beautiful. This campus is the combination of the any modern shopping mall (e.g. vibocity) and a decent forest. One can enjoyed these both feeling once he/she inside of it. Everything is heavily organized and the campus is so bug that you can not visit the entire campus in a single day. The campus is full of stairs and that's why sometimes it is called national university of Stairs.

Jia xin is one of the best tutors ive ever had!! My bio was super bad in j1 and i could only get like at most 30/100 💀

But then i started tuition with her and she was rlly patient and actually listened to identify my areas of weaknesses. She gives concise notes + answering techniques + correctly targets important topics commonly tested. The discussion during lessons and explaining concepts is also incredibly effective

Aside from that she’s also super chill and fun to talk to! At the end thanks to her help i got A for A levels!!😝

heh ok coach jia xin has been teaching me for 5 years (since p5 heh 😼) n highkey shes one of the best tuition teachers ive had 😋😋 vv easygoing, patient n fun to talk to !

i look forward to her lessons (e math n a math heh) n her explanations r easy to understand and digest,, her late night lessons r rly good as well n she tries her best to fit into ur timeslot for said lessons !
during exam period, she writes handwritten letters to encourage her students,, those said letters r what push me thru that tough period 🫶

wo her help id lwky be failing by now ok but ive been faring well in my studies n its because of her ! and even if u dont do well, she wont scold u but instead talk u thru what happened, how u can improve and how she as a tuition teacher can help u out !!

if ure looking for a teacher that can help u w math and sci, id 101 percent recommend her ! not only is she an excellent tchr who explains things rly well but shes also a very friendly person to talk to !

I still remember back when my daughter was in primary 5... We tried a few different math tutors, but she always told me that they became impatient or that she didn’t really understand their explanations. Then a friend referred me to Teacher Jia Xin.

After the first lesson with her, my daughter told me it was fun, clear, and that she didn’t feel stressed asking questions when she didn’t understand something. We decided to continue with her...and 3 years later, both my children are now in secondary school and still with her for math and sci.

Thank you Teacher Jia Xin. We really appreciate the patience and guidance you have given my kids 🙏

This teacher actually carried my math results I don't think I could ever have understood math without her. She actually goes through everything and makes sure I understand the thought process behind the methods to solve the equations !! She's also super fun to talk to and I look forward to each and every lesson 🎉 100% would reccomend

Jia Xin is a very patient, caring and kind tutor. Before attending her class, my son was unable to answer the Science OEQ but after attending her class, he is able to answer the questions well. You wont regret engaging her as your child's tutor.

place_name: Yi Xue Learning Centre (易学堂) - Clementi
review_text:
Yixue learning centre has helped my daughter
greatly with my o level results. The caring and conducive environment has allowed her to score a A1 for o level Chinese and has deepen her appreciation for the language. The teachers are very patient and are open to questions for practice papers.The centre’s well concise notes and in depth content allows her to better understand and practice on different questions while knowing the answering tips to every question. I would also like the give a huge thanks to 黄老师who has been by my her for a long time being a very patient and supportive teacher I would also like to thank 朱老师that allowed me to be well prepared for her exams and teach her very well.

place_name: Yi Xue Learning Centre (易学堂) - Clementi
review_text: I have been attending this tuition centre since i was in sec 1 and i found that the learning experience here is different from other learning centres. I attend the higher chinese class. This chinese tuition goes in dept in getting us students to understand the different writing techniques in composition, as well as the answer templates in comprehension. My teacher has been teaching me since sec 1, and she is the most patient as well as devoted chinese teacher i have encountered. Because of that, i could confidently enter the exam hall when i took the olevel norrmal chinese exam last year and managed to get an A1. Not only that, because of the intensive oral programmes organised by this tuition centre i was able to achive a distinction in my oral even though i am not a naturally good chinese speaker. Even the past years in sec 1 and sec 2, my higherchinese results have been consistently decent because of all the training we do in class. I definitely recommend this centre if your chinese standard is not high, as there are special skills taught here that will allow you to achieve your desired grade.

place_name: Yi Xue Learning Centre (易学堂) - Clementi
review_text: After only attending yixue for a year, my Chinese improved tremendously under the guidance of the teacher, helping me achieve an A1. The teaching style of the tution is easy for students with weaker fundamentals to understand and score better with a deep understanding of trends in past papers. The intensive training camps before exams were also crucial to having deeper understanding!!  thanks so much to the teachers there!!

place_name: Yi Xue Learning Centre (易学堂) - Clementi
review_text: My son joined Yi Xue Tang at the end of Sec 2 as he barely passed 高华 and 普华. He enjoyed the lessons and found the approach and exam strategies very useful. In fact, he took the O level 普华 exam at the end of Sec 3 and achieved A1! Thank you for the dedicated and good teaching by Yi Xue Tang. 🙏

place_name: Yi Xue Learning Centre (易学堂) - Clementi
review_text: We are truly grateful to 谢老师 and 朱老师 for their unwavering care and guidance. Their patience and encouragement not only helped my child achieve AL1 in the PSLE Chinese paper but also nurtured her love for learning Chinese. The well-organized materials were extremely helpful for last-minute self-revision at home. Thank you for building her confidence and making learning enjoyable. Highly recommended!

A place to study. Good air con. Open to NTU people. Lighting still ok. A lot of knowledge here but none knows how to apply or how it works. Ever studying but also brain dead at the same time.
Space quite wide ba.

Take a break Students.

Lee Wee Nam Library at NTU is hands-down one of the best places on campus to get stuff done. Whether you're cramming for finals, working on a group project, or just need a quiet place to chill with your laptop, this library’s got you covered.

The space is bright, modern, and surprisingly cozy for a university library. There’s a mix of seating — quiet study zones, comfy lounge chairs, and group discussion rooms if you need to collaborate. The views from the windows are also a nice bonus, especially if you snag a seat by the glass.

Tech-wise, it's well-equipped with plenty of power sockets, printers, and fast Wi-Fi. The online resources and book selection are solid, especially if you're in engineering or tech — not surprising, given it's right next to the School of Computer Science and Engineering.

It can get pretty packed during exam season, so go early if you want a good spot. But overall, it’s one of the more comfortable and efficient places to study on campus.

The study area on Level 5 is generally quiet and comfortable, making it a nice spot to study. However, the air conditioning is not very strong, which can make it quite warm and uncomfortable at times, especially if you're seated near the windows.

Went to the library as a visitor, not a student. But I was with a group of friends, and one of them is an NTU student. He asked if we could hang around the library as visitors. The girl who was in charge on Saturday 27/7/24 around 4 to 5pm was being very rude. She was extremely unfriendly. We were being as quiet as a mouse. But she was being very strict that we should only hang around the entrance area. This is understandable, however she could have been polite and friendly. What a fierce tigress! 4 stars only because the library environment was pretty nice and cozy. Zero star to that person in charge.
The library looks great but some spots lack AC.
A library at Frontier Community Club, within walking distance from Jurong Point. Clean and well maintained. Perfect for some quiet time.

Jurong West Public Library is a cozy and well-organized community library that’s perfect for quiet reading or studying. It offers a good selection of books across various genres, including a solid children’s section and useful resources for students. The seating areas are comfortable, and the environment is calm — ideal for concentration. Staff are friendly and helpful, and the self-service machines make borrowing and returning books easy. While it’s not the largest library in Singapore, it’s a welcoming spot for residents to read, learn, and relax.

Very nice service here. This is a 3 storey building merged with the community centre. It is accessible to the mrt through a pedestrian bridge from level3 of jurong point shopping mall. The 3 level are divided into sub categories like adult teenager and children stories. Cool to see a library integrated with the community club. Will be back again for more story books

Quiet, peaceful and cozy this place is perfect for book lovers that just one to have some quiet time alone in this quiet environment. Not only is this library used to read and borrow books, you can also bring your textbooks laptop phone here to do some revision or homework. There is also a variety of books that you can choose from. From comic books to novels and more. Highly recommended.

However it is a bit troublesome for people who drives. There is no waiting area and you have go 1 round to avoid blocking other drivers.
Nice library to spend a whole day. It has different areas for kids and adults. Most of all, it is connected to the MRT station and Jurong Point Mall. Super convenient!

Been commuting here for years, but never seen staffs up at the platform controlling the crowd during peak hours OR weekends. For eg, to ask ppl to stand at the side to let ppl OUT OF THE TRAIN FIRST, then board. :) Constant pushes of big crowds preventing ppl to go out of the train smoothly the second the doors open. Please wake up your idea! If you say there's a short of manpower, why does JE mrt always have enough staffs to control the crowd yet no one at Boonlay?

Located at nearly central of Jurong west area. Very convenient for catching train to city center. The station is attached to a huge shopping mall and the bus interchange. Highly recommended for those who work around Jurong area and Tuas.

The gantries are so user unfriendly . Entrance going in have to do a detour instead of opening some up on the side facing Jurong point.  It might be to drive traffic to the shops at mrt . But it shouldn’t be don’t at the inconvenience of commuters.

One of the busiest MRT station in Singapore.

Good thing most time seats are available. If not, better to take the train to Pioneer station and board from there going to the East.
My favourite inter-change MRT in Singapore & Double Decker Bus Stop till Cleantech.

The covered walkway beside Boon Lay mrt station is often full of litter on floor n seats daily. Cleaner removed rubbish bin n no rubbish bin now n no daily cleaning here.  A shopping trolley is filled with rubbish as a litter bin here. The rubbish is not cleared over a week now. There are pests infested here. Very unhygienic.

Pl do daily maintenance here or I report to authorities.

convenient and well-connected MRT station serving the western part of Singapore. The station is clean, organized, and easy to navigate. It provides seamless connections to nearby bus services, shopping areas, and residential neighborhoods.
The facilities are well maintained, and train services are generally efficient and reliable. A great transport hub for daily commuters and visitors alike

Boon Lay MRT Station (EW27) is a well-connected and busy station serving the western part of Singapore. It provides easy access to nearby residential areas, shopping centers, food courts, and the Boon Lay Bus Interchange.

The station is clean, well-maintained, and clearly signposted, making it convenient for daily commuters and visitors. Facilities such as lifts, escalators, and ticketing machines are readily available and functional.

Overall, Boon Lay MRT Station is a reliable and efficient transport hub that supports smooth connectivity for both work and leisure travel.
Good. But atmosphere during weekend is poor, many people gather around have drinks and chit chat around post atm and cheers shop till pick point bus stop there.

Great MRT station in the far west, and with JRL coming close we will be coming closer to having pretty much the perfect MRT, as you have ITH with 2 lines towards Choa Chu Kang, the City, the east and the JIE, TIE

The place is clean, well-organized, and easy to navigate. Buses are punctual, and the staff are helpful and polite. The waiting areas are comfortable, and the overall environment feels safe and convenient for daily commuters. Great service and well-maintained facilities—highly appreciated!

SMRT taking over Jurong West Bus Package in a few days (from 1 Sep 2024)

Air conditioned Integrated Transport Hub located next to Boon Lay MRT Station and Jurong Point Shopping Mall.

Lots of feeder services going NTU and around the western area of Singapore.  I find that ITHs are quite dim, especially the bus park. They should make the area more vibrant and play some music.

Quite crowded went there on a Monday around 6pm.

Got an unpleasant incident on January 25th last Sunday, at a snack stall called Borobudur right behind the 243G bus queue, the stall keeper was a middle-aged woman, when she wrapped my order I gave her 50$ cash the money was still stiff and new, because I had just taken it from the ATM machine, then the aunty asked for another 5$ 10 cents I thought she didn't have change so I scanned the QR barcode because I didn't have any more cash, I waited for my 40 dollar change, suddenly she said "what else ?"  , suddenly I was shocked 55.10$ for only 4 cakes and a jar of crispy fish skin was so expensive, and what didn't make sense was she said I only gave her 10 dollars, suddenly I was furious because she kept dodging she hid the money I gave her earlier after I said check the CCTV she immediately gave me the 40 dollar change with an unpleasant look, after knowing I could speak Mandarin, she thought I was stupid, the stall keeper only spoke Mandarin, I had spoken English but she refused to give me my change earlier
Ever since this SMRT take over ah the bus time all wonky and unreliable one. Everyday different time some bus also got 15 min delay for no reason one

I boarded 157 bus driven by bus captain (China . Nationality) . Total Aircon breakdown in bus on a hot day  21/11 @ 4.30pm pick up point Blk 288C Bt Batok . He panicked n do not know wat to do . Heard him communicating on the mike with the office . He cud hv got everybody to alight n together wait for next 157 bus so passengers can board without double paying .  This has been the practice I thot so . However , this was not the decision made n the driver seem to be very uneasy n seem flustered . Tis can compromise passengers safety too .

1) SBS should educate the drivers on how to handle the situation before allowing them to take the responsibility of bus captain . Our safety is in their hands .

2) Aircon breakdown - Isn’t there a maintenance check on all bus vehicles including air con maintenance before they r road worthy ?
Too much fruits shop.
Have sheltered walkway into Boon Lay Shopping Centre
Ntuc fairprice supermart.
Boon Lay Bus Interchange serves its purpose well as a major transit point. While it can get crowded during peak hours, the overall organization is commendable. Would appreciate more seating!

During my visit on 11th March 2026 to Jurong Point Bus Interchange this afternoon to report my lost Concession Card, I was struck by Ms Angela Wang Hai Yan's (B909) exceptional expertise and commitment, as she skillfully navigated me through the process with infectious enthusiasm and a solutions-oriented approach.

I took bus 174 when I to my home. Then this one person kept taking photos of me without permission and the bus driver didn’t care. It seems like the bus drivers just want to get people from A to B and don’t care about people. Then you say don’t claim a seat when I already have bad experience like this. Then I emailed the customer service they didn’t reply. It should be zero stars for the sake of it

Lighting is dark and dull, the crucial feeder bus services are perpetually having snaking lines and being placed at the full end at terminal that's often stuffy. Why? Everyday causing unnecessary time wasting for commuters having to do all the walking by the multitudes to the end! The 243G at peak times aren't serving double deckers even. The scrolling screen schedule are badly placed and turns so slowly that one has to stop to check timings than matching walking pace. The customer service are just patronising. Terminal does not match up to standards deserving in SG and the huge population in Jurong.

Boon Lay Interchange is a vital transportation hub located in the far west of Singapore, seamlessly connecting commuters to various parts of the city. Serving as a gateway for residents and students alike, it offers convenient access to Nanyang Technological University (NTU), making it an essential stop for many. The interchange is well-designed, featuring a spacious layout that minimizes congestion during peak hours, ensuring a smooth transit experience.

One of the standout features of Boon Lay Interchange is its extensive bus services, including my personal favorite, Bus 198. This bus route provides a reliable and efficient way to travel to work, weaving through the scenic routes of the western region while connecting to other key transport nodes. The frequency of buses is commendable, allowing for minimal waiting times.

Moreover, the interchange is equipped with various amenities, including food stalls and retail shops, making it a convenient spot for quick meals or last-minute purchases. Overall, Boon Lay Interchange exemplifies Singapore's commitment to efficient public transport, fostering connectivity and accessibility for all. Whether you’re a student heading to NTU or a commuter like me, this interchange serves as a crucial link to the bustling heart of the city.

Update: 14 Feb 26

Just had my first visit to the reopened Boon Lay Place Wet Market! It’s easily one of the largest and best-organized markets in Western Singapore now. The stalls are well-spaced, and the variety of items is incredibly extensive. The entire space is bright, clean, and the improved ventilation makes a huge difference. Recommended for fresh groceries.

__________________
Boon Lay Place Food Village has reopened! 😃

So glad Boon Lay Place Food Village has reopened! Just had a quick economic bee hoon breakfast there. The place looks fantastic after the 3-month renovation — brighter, more spacious, and much more comfortable than before! 👍🏻👍🏻

Recently reopened Boon Lay Food Place which has been around for the longest time.

The newly upgraded place is much more cleaner, cooler and have more space with the center segment flattened and added with more tables and seats.

Overall, the place is even brighter and houses many good food options here. When there’s a queue, typically it is good food here.

Only giving it 4-stars because the prices have all gone up, which is pretty sad as gradually hawker food do not feel as affordable as they used to be, and sadly it is a common action after any new renovations or upgrades. Just hope it is sustainable for the F&B people, as well as the patrons as most people who live around this area are much older people.

Will still frequent this place!

Although it's newly renovated hawker centre, but many areas are still required improvement, eg. cleanliness, seating, tray return etc. Majority food selling here is good and still within reasonable price, but few coffee stalls are selling drinks at higher prices compared with kopitiam or food court and quality is poor. Most stall owners are selfish and inconsiderate, they chope and reserve the tables in front of their stalls, just for the convenience of placing their stuffs, which cause diners are difficult in getting seats.

Lotsa great stalls here. They underwent renovation recently with better ventilation, cleaner environment which makes it more comfortable for patrons. My personal favourite power nasilemak, 擂茶饭，鱼汤, prawn noodle
lots of nice hawker foods with plenty of seats and tables. Many parking lots and wheel chairs accessible with toilet and fans facilities. Great for lunch & dinner.
This place is very good
Good
Great
The building is a bit old, but it's still okay. If it were repainted, it would look much better.
There are many cheap fruits for sale.

cheap, good, and very filling. The portions are generous for the price, making it a great spot when you want a satisfying meal without spending too much. The food may not be fancy, but it’s comforting and worth the value. Overall, a solid and affordable place that I’d come back to again.

I swear this will be my first and last time I am ordering rice from here.
I bought the food here purely out of necessity...but it is indeed GARBAGE!

So...I paid $10 for 3 dishes early in the morning at 7.30am
Braised beef...black wood ear fungus...tomato egg
Plus 1 packet of rice

I brought my own glass container, and I do admit the portion given is huge, but I still feel the price is steep.
In the end, I only managed to consume half portion of what was served, and toss the rest as there wasn't a fridge at work.

Last thing to add: beef was tough, as you can see how thick it was cut 👎🏻

The stalls have changed hands.  Now 100% Chinese from China.  Xian Chinese cuisines to choose from.  Min. price of the noodles starts from $6.00.  Mine is Oil Noodles (油波面），which is Xian noodles. The appetiser not cheap with the squid @ $4/- while the bean sprouts @ $1/-.  Thought they will charge $2/- for the bean sprouts.  Anyway, the noodle is not that spicy.  Most of the customers are from China.  Also can see a few Indians doing a take away or eat-in.  Will try again when around this area.

Ordered takeaway for a pork noddle dish & a bun. Total cost $10, but the portion is huge & I can eat as 2 meals. Noddles is quite decent with spicy chilli and bun is nice & crispy. Not overly salty for china dishes. Service was pleasant enough. Not bad, will come back and try others.

Buns are dope. I dream of their pork cabbage and leek egg buns all the time. Noodles not bad too but they use machine so a bit cheating ah. However one bowl of noodles enough to feed a village. I got noodles and two buns for below $10. Money worth

The food is always served so fast.

The Sambal Fried Rice is my go to option, packing with flavor and just right in terms of spiciness. The pork chop is so tender and nicely done.

So far I haven’t had a fried rice that’s better than this. Not even Din Tai Fung beats this.

My beloved Uncle 炒饭 (Fried Rice)! Come and have a taste.
Very nice coffee shop. Drinks are very good and a lot of nice beer and Indian food
I had a wonderful monuments by their service

I've never had this dish before and kept my expectations low due to the mixed reviews. It turned out better than expected. I got a total of 5 items - Chicken Drumstick, Lotus Root, Celtuce, Quail Egg, and Long Bean with Rice, for a total of $7.90.

This was super filling, even though I was very hungry. I read reviews about it being too salty or too garlicky, but it felt alright for me as I mainly ordered blanched vegetables. If you are ordering the braised items, which are already heavily seasoned, you must tell them to use less sauce. I do agree, though, that too much oil is added.

A few things I would nitpick: the lotus roots and celtuce could be sliced thinner for easier chewing. Also, they scooped out my rice way before they started preparing my items, so the rice turned slightly cold upon serving. Since the dish is lukewarm, the rice really needs to be piping hot. ​Finally, and this isn't the stall's fault, there seem to be many beer promoters in this coffee shop, which gives off slightly sleazy vibes.

I was in the area and recalled seeing this stall on social media, so I decided to give it a try. I think this is definitely worth trying if you love mala and are in the area.

Food was good albeit rather oily. Ordering pretty confusing for first-timer as i thought I have to order each ingredient with its minimum order as listed in the pricelist but they actually just weigh them per the portion you want just like ordering Mala Hotpot. Overall still quite pricey as per pax consumption at least $10 and beyond.

Went to the Tampines brunch.
Taste is very personal. Might be three star for me but someone else will enjoy it.
3 star 🌟 because I find the secret sauce very salty, 重口味for me. Also I ordered mostly the sides like long beans, tau pol and tau kee with one sausage and small cut of 猪脚肉，and the price is pretty steep.
Maybe taste is just not for me.
Tried the one at sim lim square first and it was a great meal and great experience. But tried this today at jurong east, so sorry but the oil is too heavy on this one. Couldn't enjoy it at all

Visited the new Jurong East outlet of 八方卤菜, inspired by Tik Tok videos from China. Managed to get in at 2pm, avoiding the Sunday queues I saw earlier (which were probably due to the slower chopping skills, causing a backlog). Ordered pork ear, pork nose, pork belly, Taiwan sausage, long beans, and mushrooms. Total bill came up to $26 for 2 pax, which I think is reasonable.

However, I was a bit disappointed with the taste - even with all the condiments added, it tasted like a normal braised dish, nothing special. The dish was also quite oily, lacking the fragrant spice aroma I was expecting (unlike mala Xiang Guo). Has anyone else tried this? Was it supposed to taste like this?

Had a wonderful dining experience here! The food was tasty, fresh, and served in generous portions. The staff were friendly and attentive, making us feel very welcome. The restaurant was also clean and comfortable. Highly recommended!

🇸🇬 A pet-friendly restaurant in Singapore serving TWO live crabs for only $48?!?!

It’s located at a small mall around the West Coast area, right beside a landed housing neighborhood with super convenient parking. The outdoor dining space is huge and completely pet-friendly. Honestly, the prices made me question Singapore’s cost of living 😂 Even a simple zi char meal at the market usually costs more than this, while many other restaurants charge $80+ for just ONE crab. Finally found this hidden gem!

Their BBQ is also surprisingly good with a huge variety of choices. They even have affordable solo meal sets for working adults available morning and evening — only $8.50 and it comes with a free drink! Lunch has no service charge and no GST, while dinner has additional service charges.

The zi char selection is massive too. We ordered the spicy frog legs and Thai-style sotong, both were incredibly appetizing and perfect with rice. Everything is freshly prepared and live seafood is cooked on the spot, so the ingredients taste super fresh. Definitely a great spot for gatherings if you love good value-for-money food!

📍 Yan Lin Fang, West Coast

#SingaporePetFriendlyRestaurant #WestCoastSingaporeFood #SingaporeChilliCrab #YanLinFangSingapore #SingaporeFoodie

Chanced upon this place while discovering Newest Mall. They actually had a really great deal while we were there. 2 crabs for $48, but unfortunately we were not there hungry. Ordered their Ala carte rice with tomato egg, 新洲米粉, skewers & a lamb rack which set us back around $50+. It was super good & we enjoyed ourselves very much! Definitely will be back!

the fried rice has wok hey very lovely. breathtaking place to dine in. Food is also delectable delicious and affordable.the fried egg has runny yolk which is what I like.the sambal is lovely. The waitress also asked for help.thanks for the delicious food and good service (at least they tried to communicate better with the help of the another staff member).sorry for interrupting your supper
The prices seemed reasonable but the food were tasteless and in extremely poor quality. Do note that there is service charge even though the menu indicated otherwise.

Had read the CNA story when it came out and mentally noted about the home-cafe. Finally Visited on 08-02-2026
Luckily we got seats and everyone else took it to go. We tried hot and cold matcha latte, strawberry X yogurt matcha, white coffee, Earl Gray shaken and slice of banana cake. Unfortunately the banana pudding and focaccia were sold out!
The drinks were made fresh to order and it showed in the flavour profile. I like that each drink was about enhancing the flavour with no additional funk. The hosts were descriptive with information and I thank them for the hospitality. Banana cake and matcha latte was a perfect pairing.

Asked for recommendations on coffee and matcha so we got pandan latte and strawberry x yogurt matcha, and both were really gooodd! :D

Very nice home cafe with good music chill vibes.

Love the poster outside the gate too.

P.s. Got a banana cake and it was really moist and soft!! Will be up to try their other food options next time.

(invite; but only raw and honest opinions oki)
visiting the noob coffee marks my first HBB visit in the west! 😆😆

here’s what we got!
🍵earl grey matcha (w oat milk)
the matcha’s intensity is impressive, very gao! interestingly, I found out that they actually make their own earl grey syrup. while it leans on the sweeter side so it might be a bit much for some, I found it to be the perfect counter-balance to the matcha’s earthiness. it rounds out the flavors so beautifully that you get all the richness without any harsh bitterness.

☕️dirty earl (w oat milk)
since it was our first visit, the owner recommended their Earl Grey Coffee, which is one of the Noob’s favourites! think of it as a sophisticated, 'Ang Moh' Yuan Yang!! the blend of earl grey and coffee is spot on. my sister, who is a coffee addict, is absolutely obsessed with it

Highly highly recommend their earl grey matcha latte and tnc focaccia!!! Earl grey matcha latte is super fragrant and the focaccia is really fluffy and not too dense. The owner is super nice to talk to as well and my friend and I had such a good time there ☺️ will definitely come back again!

this was not my first time eating their banana pudding, and i always find myself coming back for more! this time, i ordered it for a 10 pax christmas party and my friends finished everything 😋 it has the right amount of sweetness and i love the texture! i have also tried their coffee and matcha (original/strawberry/earl grey), in which all were great! the owners are also really friendly and welcoming :) if you live in the west, i would really recommend everyone to pop by and enjoy matcha & banana pudding at their lovely place 😗 thank you to the noob coffee once again for accomodating to my requests for my christmas party 🎄
Very economical pricing for the food sold here. But a bit too sweet and MSG added.
After I buy food, ask staff about the survey, staff show bad attitude and very rude to customer.
Bought chicken rice. The rice is so hard and dry. Chicken very hard not tender at all. Only have 3-4 pcs of chicken..  Avoid ba.. $3 is cheap but not worth it.
I don’t know why such very low rate. It was nice taste, meen Soto. Also it was friendly price.

I would give NO star if the system allows. The pricing is ridiculous ( CUT THROAT PRICING ) ! $ 8+ for a packet of plain noodle and Bee Hoon. Look at the attached photos to see how pathetic my packet of noodle and Bee Hoon looks.  Making it worse, the noodle is hard and dry and the Bee Hoon tasted extremely lousy. There is absolutely nothing worth to praise about this stall. I will never never come back again. Avoid this stall and spend your hard earned money some where else. Don't give them a chance to scam you.

I stay nearby and I find this shopping centre convenient. It offers a comprehensive range of daily necessities, from an NTUC FairPrice, coffee shops and grocery stores to hardware shops and medical clinics. It’s also a one-stop destination for services like money changers and hair salons. Notably, it houses a few travel agencies, making it an ideal spot for anyone looking to book bus trips to Malaysia.

One of my friend used the toilet here and saw shit  (poo) 💩 in the wall inside the cubicle.
