Come back to visit this place again after few years..basically not much change..the food at Hawkers Centre not back..The Power Nasi Lemak..😋

Some new deco is quite nice..

My visit to Boon Lay Shopping Centre was fantastic! 🤩 I found everything I needed and more. The stores were well-organized, and the atmosphere was very welcoming. Definitely a great spot for shopping! 🛍️✨ I'll be back soon! 👍

A lot of coffeeshop around this place and even a market where there is around 50 stalls ++.
Very good variation to choose from. Other then that alot of fruits stall and money exchange. Come early for parking space, during lunch and dinner very difficult to find parking space.

Jurong Point has really evolved into one of the largest and most comprehensive suburban malls in Singapore over the years. The mall keeps expanding and now offers an impressive variety of restaurants, cafés, retail shops, supermarkets, and leisure options all under one roof. There are also at least two sizeable food courts with plenty of local and international food choices, making it easy to find something for everyone

One of the biggest advantages is the excellent convenience - it has always been directly connected to both the MRT station and the bus interchange, making it extremely accessible rain or shine. Despite being a heartland mall, it feels vibrant and lively with many different sections to explore, from Japanese-themed food streets to family-friendly spaces and lifestyle shops

Jurong Point is one of the more convenient suburban malls in Singapore with a huge variety of shops, supermarkets, restaurants and daily necessities all under one roof. The mall is directly connected to Boon Lay MRT, so accessibility is definitely a big plus.

What I like most is the wide food selection ranging from local hawker-style eateries to proper restaurants and cafes. It’s also spacious enough for long walks without feeling too cramped on weekdays.

However, the mall can get extremely crowded during weekends and peak dining hours, especially around the food areas. Some sections also feel slightly dated compared to newer malls in Singapore, and navigating between the different wings can be a bit confusing initially.

Overall, still a very practical and convenient mall for shopping, dining and everyday errands in the west side of Singapore.

As one of Singapore’s largest suburban malls, Jurong Point serves as a massive retail and lifestyle hub for the West. It’s famous for its immersive themed precincts and endless food options, making it a hotspot for both locals and visitors.
For my family and me living in the West, Jurong Point is our go-to spot for shopping, leisure, and meals because it is just so convenient. Plus, being directly connected to Boon Lay MRT and the Bus Interchange makes it exceptionally accessible.

Didn’t expect tiramisu to hit this good, but here we are at Tirato Tiramisu location at B1 near boast.

Caved and tried two flavours — pistachio and yam — and honestly? No regrets. Just me wondering why I didn’t get more

Pistachio was rich, nutty, and somehow still light? Super creamy, but not the kind that makes you feel jelak. The subtle crunch inside? Chef’s kiss. Definitely feels a little luxe.

Yam caught me off guard — it’s soft, naturally sweet, and has that nostalgic vibe, like something you grew up eating but upgraded. Super comforting and lowkey addictive.

What made it even better:
✨ No pork, no lard, no alcohol — totally kid-friendly (my little cousin loved it)
✨ Freshly made daily — no preservatives, no colouring, nothing artificial
✨ So many flavours to choose from — I was stuck at the counter for 5 mins trying to pick. Spoilt for choice fr.

They’re only $6.80 each, which feels like a steal for the quality.
Very fun shopping at Jurong point. Lots of things to see for Chinese new year from ornaments to cookies in jars and gift boxes. Got god of fortune everywhere too. Pineapple tarts and more ~

I bought tenderloin meat from the counter that sells pork. The staff would weigh on the spot. It says 0.314g. When i reach home to portion the meat into 100g each, i only got +-230g. Where's my balance 80g??

Today (5 Sept 2025) around 1.05pm, an uncle on his PMA crashed into my trolley while we were queuing at the cashier (he was just right behind me). Super dangerous!! If he can hit my trolley, he can easily hit someone else too. I already told two NTUC staff about it. Imagine if it happened to a small kid — no parent would accept that!!

This is one of the oldest Fairprice supermarket & it is very spacious. I have been shopping here for a long time & knows exactly where to find my stuff. But with more supermarkets around, they are not as attractive than before. Other supermarkets are offering higher discounts for senior & cashback, so their prices are not as competitive now.
The most annoying thing is their self check out kiosk, which has a weight balance system on the scanned & unscan items. If you hold on to your scanned item a second longer, the system will hang & require the staff to come and reset. I suppose this is to prevent theft. But how can you treat every customer as a potential thief? This is discrimination.

Shop at boonlay shopping centre today at 11.17am, casher ask me if I have member link card which I say no, then casher use her fairprice app phone hidden at counter, without asking me permission,  very disappointed.  Why didn nobody check or advise her

Today went to FairPrice to buy Ghee on 25/03/26 at 2pm . But can’t find it . Asked the customer service centre. Old lady asked me to go lane 11 . Actually that is not there then went to lane 8  (another staff helped me). After confront the customer service lady , she doesn’t say and never sorry or anything too. Then laughing say she doesn’t know . She thought it is drinking item . Basically not suitable to customer case and useless lady .
Great product offering. Owner is really kind and helpful. Really enjoy my time shopping grocery here. Highly recommended.

Delight Supermart offers products at good value. There are many offerings that i cannot find anywhere else.

The shopowners are really kind everytime i go there, always caring and going the extra mile.
The staff was excellent in tending to my needs despite the peak hour crowd. Super patient. Also found a lot of my childhood treats there. Highly recommend!
Excellent service . Hygienic environment.Neat and fresh vegetables. Paynow and NETS accepted.Frozen food also available.My go to shop😄Must visit
Great selection of goods. Shop owner is very kind helped me find my goods

My wife recently went to this outlet.

Great space and equipment.

However the music is blasting and toilets are not cleaned well.

This has to improve. Dear AF team, do take note please
Been to a couple of their outlets and this is by far the biggest with ample equipments available. Everyone here is friendly and courteous. Highly recommend!

Spacious gym. Could do with more 5, 10 and 12.5kg dumbbells and sanitising sprays & cloths to wipe equipments and benches after using. I noticed hardly any members wipe down after using. Also noticed most lockers were locked while the gym was not full and members at that time kept their bags at the alternative wooden shelves during my visit (Sep 24, around 2pm).

Checked out Boon Lay Anytime Fitness for the first time and I’ve got to say — I’m really impressed! The gym has pretty much everything you need. Loads of machines, a wide range of equipment, and the free weights go all the way up to 50kg. The best part? They actually have two pairs of each dumbbell, so no need to fight over weights with anyone. Plenty of benches too, so you’re not stuck waiting around.

Big shoutout to Wandi at the front desk — super friendly and helpful! He made me feel really welcome on my first visit. Give that guy a pay raise!

The gym is clean and well-maintained, with four toilets/showers so it’s easy to freshen up after your workout. There’s even a vending machine with protein shakes, which is a lifesaver if you forget to bring yours.

All in all, awesome vibe, solid equipment, and great service. Definitely worth a visit if you’re in the Boon Lay area!

Spacious Neighbourhood 24hr gym. Gym manager very friendly and always smile. During weekdays after office hours can be quite busy, however crowd get lesser and lesser after 1030pm. Facilities well maintained. Enough equipments to execute most workouts.

place_name: Extreme Fitness - Personal Training Jurong East
review_text: My training experience here has been great so far and my coach has been really encouraging and motivating. I was really intimidated initially but feel more comfortable coming to the gym now :) Initially I knew nothing about fitness and gymming, but now I have become stronger and know more about various exercises. The training here is really tailored to each person’s strengths and good for beginners!  Many many many thanks to my trainer Coach Zany and also Coach Shawn :) everyone here has been really friendly and patient with me.

place_name: Extreme Fitness - Personal Training Jurong East
review_text:
Extreme Fitness Family
​It’s a super friendly environment with very nice and knowledgeable coaches! From the moment you walk in, it feels like stepping into one big family—everyone is non-judgmental and incredibly helpful. 🤝
​The gym itself is cozy and well-maintained, plus having access to the pool is a huge bonus! 🥳
​If you’re looking for real strength training, expert guidance, and the motivation to actually hit your fitness goals, this is the place to be. I’ve seen so many positive changes in myself already! 🥳
​Thank you to the whole team for the amazing training and support. Keep up the great work guys! 💪🔥

place_name: Extreme Fitness - Personal Training Jurong East
review_text: Training at extreme fitness has improved my overall form and strength. In just 20 sessions, I have gained muscle and bone strength. My trainer Kamila is very encouraging and always pushes me to do my best while guiding me in my journey. The gym equipement is well maintained and there is a wide variety to train different muscles. Overall, I have gained confidence in exercising.

place_name: Extreme Fitness - Personal Training Jurong East
review_text:
A great gym experience that combines a welcoming atmosphere from every single staff, supportive coach and a clean, well-equipped facility, fostering a sense of accomplishment.
My personal coach, Zany provide guidance, teach proper technique, and encourage me to reach my personal best.
The gym is cleaned, has well-maintained equipment and organized spaces.
Visiting them weekly helps manage stress, improves mood, and provides a space to "reset" mentally which makes you feel stronger and more empowered in daily lives.
👍🏻 to Zany and every single staff!

place_name: Extreme Fitness - Personal Training Jurong East
review_text:
I highly recommend Coach Sanjiv to anyone looking for a knowledgeable, motivating, and experienced coach. He brings a lot of expertise to every session and always makes the training fun, lively, and engaging. What I appreciate most is how he knows how to push you while still keeping the atmosphere positive and enjoyable.

Since I started training with him, I have seen a lot of real results and clear improvement in my performance and overall fitness. His guidance, energy, and commitment have made a big difference in my progress. Coach Sanjiv is truly a great coach, and I’m very grateful for everything I’ve learned from him.
Great place to play hockey games and futsal. Usually only guys here. Hope more girls can be attracted to join the sports as not much support from ActiveSG.
The futsal pitch is well maintained and the shower facilities are great

The futsal pitch is smaller and narrower than the others in SG.

The netting is really a big issue, you can't really run near them as you can easily trip over it, ie, getting your foot stuck in it, hence making the playing area much smaller than it is.

And you have to stop about 10mins before your actual time due to the closing timing. So you won't really get to play fully also.

The only good thing about this pitch  is that it is much cheaper.

Staff was not friendly and even aggressive. Specifically that one malay uncle who was pissed off after we left the futsal pitch a few minutes late, will not book the pitch again as the pitch itself is not worth the trouble and the clock is broken as well

Active SG. Two hours to kill whilst son attends a JSA close by. Senior rate @$1.50 plus 65c for motorcycle parking. Loads of gym eqpt to work everything and send you to lah lah land once you have reached back home and showered.

place_name: ActiveSG Hockey Village @ Boon Lay Gym
review_text: Very spacious and not as crowded as other active SG gyms

place_name: ActiveSG Hockey Village @ Boon Lay Gym
review_text:
Definitely one of the really good ActiveSG gyms, especially on the Western side. While you gotta follow the usual SportsSG rules, the staff are usually happy to show you around and clarify workouts. Was surprised to see them this attentive.
Can be a bit crowded during peak timings but well equipped with facilities and spacious. Would definitely recommend over pricier options.

place_name: ActiveSG Hockey Village @ Boon Lay Gym
review_text:
Just in Singapore for a few weeks and was looking for a gym where I could pay per entry. This was perfect. $3.5SGD per entry.
Better than me committing to buying a month pass at other franchises, as I wasn’t sure how much time I would have available for the gym.

Ultimately only got to the gym 3 times in 3 weeks.
Facilities have all the basics, so don’t be expecting a bougee gym, but definitely still has plenty of free weights, equipment, pylo boxes, treadmills, squat bars etc (worth noting no deadlifts allowed in this gym).
Staff were friendly too 👏🏻

Note: you MUST have a sweat towel for entry. They don’t sell any, but the market next door sells plenty for $1 if you don’t have one

place_name: ActiveSG Hockey Village @ Boon Lay Gym
review_text: Nice gym, used to come often when visiting the shopping center. but now it has very strong chemical smell after renovation. It has been a few months, but inside the smell is still strong and i cannot bear for more than 20 mins. Not sure whether the smell is harmful to health.

place_name: ActiveSG Hockey Village @ Boon Lay Gym
review_text:
Although ActiveSG shows the gym capacity as only 35% full, there were actually many people in the gym. So I wonder how did the system compute the capacity. It’s rather misleading.

Anyway, I would avoid peak hours from 530pm to 8pm. Especially every Singaporean has $100 ActiveSG vouchers to spend. So it makes the gym more crowded now.

The kids playground is close until further notice. No info online. If u intend to go cos of playground better find ways to check with pool first.
No wonder playground look so quiet. I peep in to confirm there is playground before buying tickets. But in the end can't use playground.

Note! It's Feature pool price. I buy under normal cannot scan. Counter say it's feature pool.

I'm a regular at the Jurong East swimming complex but the facilities are either not opened or old so I decided to take two more stops to Pioneer MRT Station where Jurong West swimming complex is.

Just 5 to 10 minutes walk from the train station, Jurong West Swimming Complex share the same building with alot of childcare, enrichment Centre, basically kids friendly place and also a stadium.

It has 2 swimming pool, one of the them is about 0.9 m, suitable for kids who don't know how to swim. The play area is not too bad, 2 slides but not for adults and anyone above 1.2 m. Too bad the bigger slide and lazy pool is not opened. Oh yes, there is a jacuzzi by the side for adults.

Overall, the facilities are very well maintained.

The staff are very nice and extremely helpful in our situation. We had wanted to go swimming in Singapore and remembered that a few years ago, all you had to do was tap your bus card to enter.

However, things have changed- now, in order to enter at all, you have to register for an ActiveSG account. Unfortunately for non- Singapore residents, this process is nearly impossible without a Singaporean phone number, and then also a Singaporean credit card.

Thankfully, we had helpful desk staff figure out a way to sneak through the problem, and one staff offered to use his credit card in exchange for payment in cash. We still needed to provide the postal code for our flat, but eventually, after almost half an hour of trying, we managed to get through without registering for an account.

Then, when we got inside, we realised that the entire waterslide and lazy river had been closed because of no one doing regular maintenance during Covid!

Not only that, but even though we paid for adult passes inside, the kids were not allowed into the jacuzzi because they said only adults. And the kids were not allowed on the kid's playground either because they had to be very short in order to enter.

All that said, we enjoyed swimming in the pool even though we could have went to the pool next to our flat, but we thought this would be more fun with a waterslide.

We are very dissatisfied with the system, but the only reason for 2 stars is the staff were helpful, otherwise we would have had to go home completely.
Cheap entry, normal ticket, not feature pool ticket. The big slide is quite long and the kids love it. There is also a lazy river.

Inexpensive place to spend time with family, friends or even by oneself. Big enough place with more than 4 pools move around including a huge slide. Children can play in the water playground with slides for children under 1.2m, so not much quarrel there. Olympic size indoor pool for more serious swimmers. A lazy pool just to laze around, tubes for rental available at counter.
I took my granddaughter for swimming classes here. She is six years old and just enjoys the class. The coach is good. The pool is well maintained.

Golden Village is a reliable cinema option in the west, located conveniently within Jurong Point near Boon Lay MRT. The cineplex has multiple screening halls with comfortable seating and good screen and sound quality, making it suitable for regular movie outings.

Ticketing is efficient with self-service kiosks and automated entry gates, which helps reduce waiting time during peak hours. The cinema typically screens a wide range of movies, from mainstream releases to popular blockbusters.

The vibe is casual and family-friendly, though it can get bustling during weekends and evening shows. Being inside Jurong Point also means easy access to food outlets and shops before or after your movie.

A practical choice if you’re looking for a straightforward and comfortable cinema experience in the Jurong area. 🎬🍿

Watched hoppers last night, last showtime of the day. Ordered a mix of sweet and salted popcorn, but some of the popcorn tasted weird, like antiseptic. It did have a numbing sensation. And it was mostly from the left side of my popcorn box (not sure if this even matters). Thankfully we already read the other review before we visited, so we stopped ourselves from consuming the rest of the popcorn 💔💔 Never had such issues with other GV outlets… please QC!!

24 Nov 2025, Went for Jurong GV, 11am Wicked for Good. Delay for 39 mins, picture of not align, seeing blur movie, later got picture no music, later got movie no picture. Could not finish movie because of delay. What GV is trying to do? Look more like a scam... Need to report to CASE for such issue. It so irresponsible. Spoil the whole program for the family days. Some audience left.

At 12pm, the staff ask the rest of the audience whether they would like to move to another theater to see the movie properly.

Haven’t been to the cinema in forever and finally went to catch Zootopia 2 but the seats smells here (Theatre 4). Wonder if it’s because not much people watch movies these days so the hygiene level dropped?

3 stars cause seat is pretty comfy and there’s Ijooz now which you can bring into the theatre! It’s priced $1 higher than other machine tho. GV toilets v big and clean.

It was very disappointing this time. I have come to golden village a lot of times before but this has not happened before. There was this staff who shouted at me when I was buying the popcorn. We said sweet for the popcorn but ended up scooping salty. Hence we had to stop her and tell her it was sweet. However she had already done it so we had to get mixed or salty. And she shouted at us whether sweet or salty in a very disrespectful manner. In the end we had to get mixed. It was a very a disappointing experience.

Thank you for taking over Cathay! I really enjoyed the Premiure Class experience. Food ordering was easy and steamless. The seat are spacious and comfortable. Thank you for taking over Cathay — the Premiere Class experience at Shaw Theatres was truly impressive 👏

From the moment we stepped in, everything felt elevated. The ordering process was seamless and fuss-free, making the whole experience feel smooth and well thought out.

The seats were incredibly spacious and plush, perfect for fully relaxing throughout the movie. What really stood out was the thick, cozy blanket — a small touch, but it made a huge difference in comfort and warmth.

Overall, it’s a premium cinema experience that delivers on both comfort and convenience. Definitely worth it for a more luxurious movie night — will be back again! 🎬✨

First of all, thank you Shaw for taking over Cathay and giving us West-siders a proper cinema again! Super grateful to have a nice place nearby to catch movies. I want to commend the team for transforming the space into a much more comfortable and movie-vibe environment compared to the previous operator (specifically the hallway and toilets). Took many photos during my trip.

I recently had the opportunity to experience the Premiere halls (specifically Premiere 1 & Premiere 2). I understand that this is a new team and they are still familiarising themselves with the SOPs. I can see they are trying their best. Still, I would like to share both the positive aspects and areas for improvement based on my visits.

What I Loved:
1) I tried the seafood pizza & it was delicious. Although the price is on the higher side, the quality justifies it. The food definitely stands out as one of the highlights of the Premiere experience.

2) The adjustable sofa chairs are very comfortable & the overall privacy in both Premiere halls is excellent. It really elevates the experience and makes the upgrade feel worthwhile.

3) When I caught The Predator at Premiere 2 hall, the sound was loud, immersive & clear. Exactly what I expect from a premium hall. Sadly wasn’t the case for Premiere 1 hall.

Areas of Improvement:
1) The lighting for the seat numbers is very bright in Premiere 1 hall (refer to photo) and was distracting during the movie. Dimming them slightly would improve the overall ambience.

2) Because the team is new, I understand they’re still adapting but there were noticeable inconsistencies:
- Premiere 2 hall, I was given cutlery and tissue with my pizza. Premiere 1 hall, I wasn’t.
- I encountered issues with both Premiere halls: once I couldn’t use the discount code and another time my order didn’t arrive until staff repeatedly came back and forth to resolve it.
The staff are young and clearly trying their best but the service processes may need refinement so the experience feels smooth and premium.

3) When I watched Wicked in Premiere 1, the audio sounded muffled and soft, especially from the front speakers. This was quite noticeable and very different from my excellent experience in Premiere 2. I’m not sure if this was due to the sound mix provided for the film or the hall setup but the inconsistency between the two halls was obvious.

Despite the hiccups, the improvements to the cinema are significant & I appreciate having such a comfortable well-designed place close to home. Just need adjustments to service consistency and technical setup (JEM please don’t disappoint Shaw good sound system reputation).

Thank you.

Very clean and movie quality very clear and good!👍🏻✨
Cinema here is new so come check it out while it's still very new can clean🤣🤣
Overall very nice, staffs also very friendly. Bought tickets through machine easy to use.
👍🏻✨👍🏻👍🏻✨✨👍🏻✨👍🏻👍🏻✨

my first time at shaw theatres jem! very clean and the seats were really comfortable
i was surprised by the sound system here, it’s really really good, even for a normal digital screening! out of the three times i’ve watch project hail mary this was by far the best experience
5 stars 🤩

first time coming to jem for a premium movie and it was a good experience. we only got fries but i was surprised they had ice creams and alcohol available too. the whole atmosphere is calm with dim lighting, plush seats and tons of legroom. it’s super family friendly but still feels grown up so you can actually relax. kamalii gave us a quick tour and explained all about the premium options so we knew what we were getting. the premium waiting area looks excellent and the service was really good, way better than the other outlets i’ve been to. she made us feel like vips, not just another booking. we’re definitely coming back here soon.

We brought our little one to the KidSperience @ Boon Lay event 12 Oct 2025, and it was such a meaningful and fun-filled day! ❤️

The kids had a great time exploring different careers — from doctor and banker to firefighter and more — through hands-on role play. It was amazing to see their excitement and curiosity as they learned and imagined what they could be in the future! 🌟

A big thank you to Boon Lay Community Centre and everyone involved for organizing such a wonderful event. The effort, creativity, and attention to detail truly showed, and we’re so grateful for the unforgettable experience. 🙏

Hope to see more of these amazing events in the future — they’re such great opportunities for children to learn, grow, and most importantly, have fun! 💕
Cashier is friendly and polite. Serve me well and fast respond in my order. Best place to dine in or take away. Great service.

I went in to level 2 and level 3 of the CC. there are a lot of enclose room even the door see thru portion are blocked with papers . Highly suspicious rooms are use for other purpose . The authority should audit those rooms 😂

It is call
Community Center but there are no residents activity in a normal week day , except the hall
Has a Chinese martial
Session ongoing . There is no reading corner no space for nearly residents to mingle or take a break..  what is the purpose of a community Center ? Should add an Aging Center also

Community events in area are held here. Some courses available at nominal fees. Place feels cramped and small, limited seating space for interaction and limited parking lots nearby. Basketball court and indoor air conditioned badminton hall took most of ground floor space. There is a non manned counter posbank machines besides admin office. Public toilets and WiFi available.

Pizza hut is here and several ATMs, basketball court, study room, multi purpose hall that can turn into badminton court and a nice auditorium. They usually arrange nice programs for the boon lay residents. Near by plenty of food courts. St Francis of Assisi church is next to it.

Sunday morning rain was falling on the ground as I walked into this temple-looking building and realised it was Yuhua Community Centre and Jurong East Neighbourhood Police Centre. It is compact and versatile although it doesn't have many amenities. There is a vending machine, table tennis, and three basketball courts to make up for the inconvenience. Whatever it is, in my opinion, this venue is quiet and relaxing.
Clean toilets and well maintained building. Badminton courts were nice and bright but not glaring to the eyes.
Lovely place, with a small garden and courts. First time I went here got a little confused but I got the hang of it. The multi-purpose hall was huge. Love it:)
Friendly people and very well organized!

First time came to YHCC, for promote n sharing about C2C program. This is a place for community to having fun, learning together, surprisingly they do  have a small canteen selling local food in reasonable price, is better then other CC only selling on fast food or  restaurant.
Playground

Forest Ramble is a fantastic children’s play wonderland nestled in nature. The themed play zones — Monkey Play, Squirrel Play, and Snake Play — are creatively designed and encourage lots of climbing, balancing, and imaginative play. It feels adventurous yet thoughtfully integrated into the surrounding greenery

The playground is well laid out with different zones catering to kids of various ages, making it easy for children to explore while adults supervise comfortably. Do note that some sections may be under maintenance at times, but there’s still plenty to keep kids entertained
A beautiful relaxing park and with nice playground. Except that some parts are closed for maintenance since 7th July 2025. That’s quite a long time for maintenance….

One of the most exciting children outdoor playground.
Plenty of different play stations.
Swings, spring jumps, slides, flying fox, mini trampoline, adventure bridges, rope runs, see saws and many many more.
Exciting moments for kids age 5 to 12 years old and 2 to 5 yrs old with adult supervision.
Shaded area, water cooler, drinks vending machine and toilets are available.
Opening hrs:
8:00am - 10:00pm,
Closed on Mon (except for PH) and Tue if preceding Mon is a PH

This was a great place to bring my nephews and cousins. The playground is spacious, fun, and has a lot of different areas to explore, so it kept everyone entertained. It’s well designed, safe, and a really nice outdoor spot for families to spend time together.

Very nice playground with lot of play setup for kids from swings to slides. Jumping climbing everything was there in the playground. On Rainy days play equipments are not allowed to use until dry. Totally recommended

A pleasant neighbourhood park that’s generally well maintained and easy to enjoy. The greenery is neatly kept, paths are clean, and there’s a good mix of features like open lawns, walking paths, small water features, and basic fitness and play areas. It’s a comfortable space for a casual walk, light exercise, or a short break outdoors

The park is relatively small compared to some of Singapore’s larger parks, so it doesn’t take very long to explore. That said, its compact size also makes it convenient and accessible, especially if you live or work nearby and just want a quick escape into nature without travelling far

Jurong Central Park · Singapore ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

Visited in the morning and found it fairly quiet, which made for a pleasant short stroll. The park isn’t particularly spectacular, but it’s a nice spot for light walks and a quick escape into greenery. With the extension currently closed off, the full loop is relatively short at just under 1 km.

What makes the park distinctive are its playful, game-inspired features like the life-sized Snakes and Ladders playground and Ludo garden, alongside ponds and shaded paths. While not a must-visit, it’s a creative and family-friendly community park that adds variety to the West’s green spaces.

Weekend Wanders: Jurong Central Park
Took a stroll through Jurong Central Park over the weekend — a peaceful green escape nestled just minutes away from Jurong Point and Boon Lay MRT.

Though it’s not as expansive as Jurong Lake Gardens, the park offers a refreshing and lush environment that’s perfect for unwinding after a hearty lunch or shopping spree at the mall. With its open lawns, shaded walking paths, and the gentle rustling of trees, it’s a wonderful spot to just pause and breathe.

If you’re around Jurong and need a short nature break, this park is definitely worth a visit. Sometimes, it’s the smaller green spaces that offer the biggest reset.

The park is steeped in history and local knowledge about plants if you know who to look for. Overall also easily 3 degree Celsius cooler.

I spotted a local water hen having a blast and thought it's cute

Jurong Central Park is a well-maintained, family-friendly park in Singapore, featuring creative play areas for children and ample space for walking, running, and jogging.

Some visitors have noted its location in the western part of the island, which may be less convenient for travelers staying in other areas.

Overall, the park is appreciated for its clean environment and suitability for various outdoor activities.

review_text:
Rifle Range Nature Park is a great extension to the Bukit Timah nature area, offering a mix of easy trails, lush rainforest scenery, and interesting glimpses into Singapore’s natural and historical heritage. The park is well connected, with clearly marked paths, boardwalks, and links to nearby nature reserves

One unexpected highlight was spotting a durian tree - and even seeing fallen durians on the ground, a reminder of how rich and diverse the forest ecosystem is. Along the trails, you’ll also find impressive giant trees, dense vegetation, quarry views, and remnants of the area’s past hidden among the greenery

I loved Rifle Range Nature Park — it’s very friendly for children and the elderly. The boardwalks are well-paved, anti-slip, and mostly barrier-free, so it’s easy for prams and seniors to walk comfortably.

The access is super convenient: just take Beauty World MRT Exit A, go to Level 4 of Beauty World Centre, and cross the overhead bridge that leads directly into the park. Highlights include the Quarry Wetland, Colugo Deck, and a fun Forest Exploration Trail for kids. Highly recommended for a relaxed family nature walk.

Nice new park with natural trails and paved paths. Has many unspoilt flora and fauna, saw several durian and starfruit trees along the paths. Not so easy to get to if depending on public transport. Toilets and carpark are available and some paths r equipped with CCTV! Not crowded on a weekday morning.

A nice trek away from the hustle and bustle of the city located in the Bukit Timah area of Singapore. Amidst a lush green rain forest with thriving ecosystems Easy to access and to reach on foot and public transport from the nearest MRT station called Beauty World - Downtown Line (Blue Coloured Line). An overhead bridge links one of the entrances of the park to the roof top Food Court (highest floor) of Beauty World Shopping Centre. Easy enough for amateur trekkers, family outings, a stroll in the park or a jog. There is a main building for meeting area, car drop off and pick up, giant ceiling fans to cool you down, chilled drinking water dispenser, vending machines, maps, loo and a park ranger on duty.  Good place to shelter if it rains There are some slopes on the way up to the cliff top of the old quarry with a stunning viewing Birds Eye area perched right at the top of the cliff. Not a long distance but short slightly steep slopes but totally worth the climb. Saw families with kids around the age of 5 and above at the top so it’s really not that challenging or difficult. Also Not as far as I expected. But do take note of your way back because if you take a wrong turn and end up on Rifle Range Road it’s more than a 1 hour walk to the nearest MRT station especially if you are already tired. However the paths are all well paved and safe. Do note the park closes at 7pm. You can also access this park from the Green Corridor.

About mid range level of difficulty. Lot of monkey though. Place is very peaceful and the lookout area is scenic. In awe. It’s connected to Rail Corridor as well, that one is pretty easy in difficulty level. It’s a literally straight road. Just need endurance for the distance.

🫛🍅Boon Lay Nature Garden is the result of the Edible Garden initiative started by the Foodscape Collective, with support from NParks.
🌽🥬The Collective aims to transform under-utilized green spaces in parks to community gardens.
🌿It was first named as Biodiverse Edible Garden at Jurong Central Park before the resident regular community adopted its present name.
🍂🪹The Boon Lay Nature Garden makes its own compost, with help from the Collective.
A wonderful place for plants lover to learn and work on gardenings👩🏻‍🌾🥰🥳✨
Lovely regenerative garden

It is well maintained. It is designed to be a place where everyone can relax for a while. There is a playground for the little ones, a gym for the adults, a walking ground. A park with a lily pond for those who love nature. An open field for entertainment. McDonald's for food. Drinking water facility, toilet facility, car parking. A separate place for parking bicycles. Nearby is the Poonle railway station. The place is beautifully located as a mall.

It's been such a long time since I last visited the Chinese Garden. So much has changed. Evening walk till night and managed to get two different scenery of the same place. Very quiet with few joggers and walkers. Interesting new features are added here. Saw a magpie calling for friends and a stork having a stroll. White crane feeding at the open field from the mrt station. Surprised to find a little bamboo lane leading to a waterfall.

Nice scenic garden in the west, conveniently accessible via public transport and a network of cycling pathways linked to its entrances. Completely free of charge as well. Best to come here on weekday evenings for better weather and less crowd.

A scenic and serene park built in the style of northern Chinese imperial architecture and landscaping. It is just a short walk away from either Chinese Garden or Lakeside MRT Stations. The key highlights of the Garden are the 13 arch White Rainbow Bridge, the Grand Arch building, the Stone Boat and Tea Pavilion, the pagodas and the gorgeous lotus pond. You may get to see little animals like birds, snakes and others thriving in the park

Very spacious garden/park area! Highly recommend coming during sunset as it sort of cools down and there’s more shade.

I’d come on a weekday to avoid crazy crowds of people sitting and talking at specific spots.

There’s an ice cream shop and a cafe in the park areas as you walk around!

It’s very clean and suitable for all ages! There are bikers and runners so keep your ears open as people are going pass you!

A Tranquil Masterpiece in the Heart of the West

The newly reopened Chinese Garden is simply stunning. Every detail — from the traditional architecture to the lush landscaping — feels thoughtfully designed and beautifully maintained. It’s like stepping into a serene, storybook version of ancient China, right here in Singapore.

The paths wind gently through water lily ponds, stone bridges, pavilions, and tranquil courtyards. The garden feels spacious but intimate at the same time, with quiet spots to sit and reflect, and picture-perfect views everywhere you look. You can tell that immense care has gone into preserving the cultural elements while making it inviting for all visitors.

It’s not just a place to walk — it’s a place to slow down. Whether you’re into photography, nature, or just want a peaceful escape from city life, the Chinese Garden delivers a truly magical experience.

I can’t recommend it enough — one of Singapore’s most beautifully restored gems.

🚤⛵🛳️This Promenade Lookout Jetty with a sweeping view of the stunning shoreline, often moored with ocean-going ships, small colourful fishing boats, stunning yachts and even Jackup Oil Rigs is a popular spot for fishing enthusiast, as well as a great spot to view the sunset.
🏝️West Coast Park which was built on reclaimed land was experiencing coastal erosion, in the early days.
📥To alleviate the erosion at the shoreline, a granite barrier, along with a picturesque promenade was built.
🎣🛟A Lookout Jetty was also included along with the scenic Promenade.

The Promenade Lookout Jetty at West Coast Park is a hidden gem offering a panoramic coastal view that stretches from the Republic of Singapore Yacht Club to Pasir Panjang Terminal and Jurong Island. It's a perfect spot for watching sunsets, with the serene atmosphere enhancing the experience. Photographers will appreciate the expansive views and peaceful surroundings, making it an ideal location for capturing stunning waterfront scenes. The jetty is rarely crowded, providing a tranquil escape where you can relax and take in the beauty of the coast.

Good:

1. A small quiet pier at West Coast Park.
2. Good view of Singapore Port.
3. Golden sunsets on non cloudy days..
4. Police cctv camera give a sense of security.
5. Police patrol duty every  night is very reassuring.
6. Great opportunity for photo ops and drone videography.

Bad:

1. Not a scenic beach. The view in the front is spoiled by cranes and ships.
2. Jetty is very short. Doesn't take you far into the sea.
3. Not ample shelter around. Need to run in case of sudden showers / thunder storms.
4. Do not go down into the water unless an expert swimmer. It is deceptively deep esp during high tide.
5. Lower rocks are exposed during low tide. Don't go down as these can be as slippery as ice.

It is a small spot inside the west coast park, where you can sit on a bench and look the harbour. You nearly have the same view as along the normal border path (along the harbour and on the side of the playground area). In the first picture: how you reach the spot. In the second one: the view
My first time to see super boat on the port. The area fits for circling and running, pinic also a good choice
