Met an old friend here. The place looks nice. We lost in touch soon after. Pity
viewed two units. location not bad, but the workmanship is terrible. it is ridiculous that a 5 years condo has peeling tiles everywhere and broken sewage filter here and there.

It is a cosy place to stay. Ambience of a resort. Without telling you more, you will be thinking as if you are staying in resorts such as  Phuket, Bali, Pattaya or even Seychelles. Reasonable size function room with nice blueish swimming pool coupled with nice beautiful clouds. The units of each apartments ceiling is about 3m high. Behind it has a big n nice public park. Built n designed by World Class but minimum it a class of its uniqueness. Hard to come by with nice and big landscape. Nowadays it is difficult to find a condominiums with such a big and scenic landscape.
Quiet and simple straight path

Ulu Pandan Park Connector is a well-loved green corridor in the West, stretching alongside the Ulu Pandan Canal. It links key residential areas like Ghim Moh, Clementi, and Pandan Gardens, while connecting to other major trails like the Rail Corridor and Bukit Batok Park Connector.

It’s a great route for walking, running, cycling, and even birdwatching with wide, well-paved paths and plenty of greenery along the way. The connector also passes by several small parks — including Faber Heights Park — and offers occasional rest spots and fitness corners. While not the most scenic path in town, it’s peaceful and practical, with good connectivity and a strong community vibe.

I’d rate it a 4 out of 5. It’s functional, accessible, and enjoyable for daily exercise or a weekend ride.

Amazing place to walk/bike.

Lots of trees for cover and separate lines for walking/cycling.

There are a lot of people especially during evenings and weekends, but there’s enough space for everyone.

The views of the river are beautiful and you can hear and see a lot of birds.
A nice place for jog or walk. After part of the PCN closure (due to the landslide at the construction site of BTO), there are lesser cyclists passing through this section of the connector.

This is quiet PCN that runs along the canal, large enough to fit all mobilities, on both sides of the canal. There are breaking up to cross roads but only a few easy to cross (no pedestrian bridge)
You will also find an old railway bridge and a spot where eagles stay around.

Too much air pollution from fumes generated from PUB work around the canal especially Clementi Road, Sunset Way and Ulu Pandan Road junction. Also noise in the evenings can be heard by residents living in the area.
The environment is both expansive and conducive to fostering a balance between academic pursuits and recreational activities for students.
Teachers are mostly nice and helpful. School is very peaceful. Though i wished it was a little more hyped and had fixed classrooms.

super rowdy students. A bunch of 6 gals in NYJC PE attire was making a lot of noise and shouting/ talking loudly to each other across table as if they owned the japanese restaurant at NEX.. the old lady next to their table told them to quiet down, but they went back to super noisy level within a min. tye old lady gave up.. the 6 gals totally disregarded the comfort level of other people eating at the restaurant. they finished their food long ago but still stayed back to continue their rowdy behaviour at the restaurant. though the japanese restaurant is not a quiet place, their noise level was so deafening high that their voices can be heard across many tables. they need social etiquette lesson badly.

Inarguably the most humiliating experience of my life. Mental health is a joke to them and teachers are incredibly biased. If you don't score well, they'll scold you saying its a problem with the effort you put in and not learning gaps that teachers need to help with. Is this the "supportive" environment required for students who already feel like they are struggling to the point of despair?!

Don't even get me started on the insane workload yet little guidancefrom tutors during the lessons. If you're not a top student,  you can forget about it (portfolio opportunities included). The "mugging culture" is useless if your teachers do not support you.If your grades for o lvl lets you go any of the other top jcs (ej hci ri, no I am not affiliated) please just go there. Trust me it's going to be a really tough 2 years if you're not acing all your exams from the jump. I sincerely hope what I experienced is the worst case scenario for all prospective students, but I'm letting you know what that looks like.

Located at Lorong Chuan. This JC is affiliated to Chung Ching High School and it is considered to be, and I stand corrected, to be the top 5 JC in Singapore. Their official uniform is light beige in color.

A Dynaglass 20DX off Singapore Poly Marina with students onboard. This boat is the most popular boat for the practical lesson of MPA approved Powered Pleasure Craft Driving Licence (PPCDL) course.

Google MPA website for the "Introduction to Pleasure Craft Licensing" if you are keen to pursue PPCDL.

Some of the best times in my poly life we’re spent here. This is the only MPA approved PPCDL test center and is also home to SMTC global offshore training. Numerous Co-curricular clubs from the polytechnic also share this place, notably the SP sailing club. Classes are conducted here mainly for students of the Singapore Maritime Academy
No parking place, just road side parking. Power pleasure boat license practical test held here.
Couched in the corner beside RSYC is Poly Marina. If you are taking PPCDL your test is here.
STCW courses, PPCDL Course/Test Centre, Offshore Courses and etc.

SUSS has been a meaningful part of my academic journey, especially since it was the only institution that accepted my application. While the curriculum is undeniably rigorous, it has also pushed me to grow intellectually and adapt to increasingly complex expectations, particularly as I approach Year 3. Some assignments are quite challenging and require a high level of critical thinking, which can be demanding but ultimately rewarding.

One concern, however, is the school’s efforts to combat the use of AI. While the intention is understandable, the system has flagged a significant number of false positives, creating unnecessary stress and anxiety among students. This has, at times, affected the overall learning experience and assignments submission. I hope that the institution can fine tune and resolve this issue soon.

Additionally, certain components such as the e-portfolio—especially in the last semester—feel like an added chores for students. Instead of adding meaningful value to learning, it can come across as repetitive and disconnected from more practical or relevant academic outcomes. Suss might want to review if Eportfolio is truly necessary.

Despite these challenges, SUSS remains a good institution that offers a strong platform for preparing students for the realities of the working world. Its emphasis on applied learning and resilience equips students with practical skills that extend beyond the classroom.

SUSS, some jokingly called it Singapore University of Self-Study due to its nature of promoting adult learners who are capable of doing their own independent learning to improve classroom discussions, are also known as the Singapore University of Stairs and Slopes now due to their recent upgrades by me. I used to not find the trail to classroom not so physically challenging until recently, maybe it’s just me :)

The Singapore University of Social Sciences (SUSS), vibrantly anchored at 463 Clementi Road, is a truly magnificent phenomenon in the educational landscape that I find deeply inspiring for its unwavering commitment to "Head, Heart, and Habit." Having engaged in numerous enlightening dialogues with passionate social workers and empathetic human resource leaders in my network, I am genuinely fascinated by how SUSS operates as a high-fidelity, merytorical sanctuary where academic rigor is inextricably linked to community impact. My acquaintances often emphasize that the pedagogical energy within its Clementi campus is exceptionally inclusive, offering a rare environment where lifelong learners and young undergraduates alike are empowered to master complex counseling or supply chain systems under the guidance of socially-conscious mentors. Public sentiment remains remarkably high, viewing the university not merely as an adult learning center, but as a visionary architect of a talent pipeline that remains a dominant force in the 2026 silver economy and non-profit sectors. Reflecting on these recurring, authentic testimonials from peers who have transformed their local neighborhoods, I perceive this institution as a distinguished powerhouse of human progress that executes its mission with a level of humanitarian audacity and Singaporean pragmatism that is truly refreshing. This academic landmark is consistently described by those "in the know" as a place where the theoretical sparks of social theory are transformed into the tangible steel of community resilience. Furthermore, the university's pioneering focus on applied learning ensures that graduates enter the labor market as highly specialized, compassionate assets with a distinct ethical edge. I personally admire the way SUSS fosters a culture of mutual respect and social responsibility, treating every student as a future steward of a more equitable society. The campus itself is often praised for its accessible, collaborative atmosphere, providing a professional backdrop that mirrors the inclusive environments our future social pioneers are destined to lead. It is heartening to hear from my network that the university’s flexibility is not just a logistical feature, but a core philosophy that respects the diverse life paths of its students. Many of my colleagues point out that SUSS’s industry-integrated projects provide an invaluable bridge to the real world, ensuring that theoretical knowledge never stays trapped in a book. I find it remarkable how the institution maintains its status as a premier public university while remaining so grounded in the everyday needs of the people. Every conversation I engage in regarding this university reinforces my belief that it is an extraordinary engine of social mobility and lifelong empowerment. The school’s dedication to the "Humanities for the 21st Century" is, in my view, the definitive blueprint for an education that values people over mere profit. Ultimately, aligning one’s academic trajectory with the Singapore University of Social Sciences is regarded as a strategic masterstroke for anyone determined to navigate the complexities of the modern world while remaining part of a supportive, high-caliber intellectual family. This institution stands as a brilliant testament to the power of education when it is fueled by a genuine desire to serve the greater good.

School admins are mostly very unhelpful. Any issue made over calls are often handled poorly by people who never know how to direct me to the right person and try to handle it themselves. Finding the right person to communicate with is sometimes difficult because of this.

School rarely takes responsibility for their actions and often shoves responsibility onto students. They will hardly put in effort to try and properly understand any issues you have
If "standard procedures" solutions does not help solve your issue then that is the extent of their help they bother to offer. They even suggest solutions which clearly doesnt help solve the issue at hand and sometimes completely irrevelvent to the issue due to misunderstanding the issue.

would not recommend enrolling here.
SUSS promotes lifelong learning with programmes that support academic and professional growth. The campus is modern and provides a collaborative environment for students.

place_name: I Love Learning Achievement Center (Clementi West)
review_text: Highly recommend ILLAC! My child has been with ILLAC for a few months now with the guidance of teacher Jerolin. Teacher Jerolin is a really great teacher, she knows how to make lessons fun and is really encouraging & supportive! Teacher Jerolin also knows how to bring back my child's focus on learning in a positive and fun way whenever my child is distracted! She is the main reason my child my child looks forward to class weekly & loves reading too! My child excels in other areas as well eg motor skills, numeracy etc as ILLAC also covers these areas! In less than a year, my 4 year old child turning 5 this year is able to read on her own! Grateful for ILLAC & teacher Jerolin! 🥰💖

place_name: I Love Learning Achievement Center (Clementi West)
review_text:
Both my daughters are currently attending ILLAC Clementi West Centre. We first enrolled my younger daughter when she was in Primary 1. After an assessment, Teacher Bridget advised her to start from Level 2. At that time, my daughter didn’t even know her sight words.
In just 8 months, she could read independently and solve Maths sums on her own with minimal guidance. From knowing almost nothing, she can now read Primary 2 books confidently! Teacher Bridget is a very dedicated teacher who truly understands her students needs and always brightens up my daughter’s day. My daughter absolutely loves attending her classes.
After seeing such great progress in my younger girl, we decided to enroll my elder daughter ( in Primary 4) for Maths, as she was struggling and usually scored around 60+. We enrolled her around August, and Mrs Wee became her teacher.
In the very first class, Mrs Wee identified my daughter’s eye issue and her difficulties with Maths. Knowing this, she patiently taught her finger methods to double-check her work, explained her careless and transfer errors, and made sure she fully understood each question before ending class.
Mrs Wee even sent me videos to help with my daughter’s eye exercises. Her care goes beyond teaching she’s not just a teacher, but also like a therapist who truly understands her students challenges. Thanks to her guidance, my daughter scored 91/100 in her year-end exam!
The word “thanks” alone isn’t enough to express my gratitude to both teachers. A special mention also to the admin team they are always friendly, approachable, and ready to assist with any issues.
ILAC has the best team, and I feel truly blessed to have found this centre.

place_name: I Love Learning Achievement Center (Clementi West)
review_text: Attending I Love Learning Achievememt Center (illac) is definitely a great choice. Within less than a semester in illac,  my daughter has made significant improvement especially in her creative writing. It has not only earned her the high achiever recognition,  it has also boost her confidence. Most importantly,  she enjoys and looks forward to her weekly lesson in illac which motivates her to work even harder!

place_name: I Love Learning Achievement Center (Clementi West)
review_text: Both my kids attended ILLAC. Thank you, teacher Bridget.

place_name: I Love Learning Achievement Center (Clementi West)
review_text: At I Love learning, I have learned many things that not all tuition centres teach. They even went an extra mile to have a star redemption to motivate students to do their work well.

Spacious JE Centre Library with many seats availability. Cool Air-Cond Environment, Front Welcoming Display, Information,  Photo Wall Taking, Green Wall Digital Huge Screen & Clean well maintained Toilet. Great Place for Reading, Desktop PC Computer Internet access & comfy sofas. Wonderful!👏🇸🇬

Outstanding library. Convenient location (OPP Science Centre) and many floors or materials. Their program room is great for kid activities.

Surprisingly, toilets are badly maintained :(
Nice, quiet and with lots of books. Highly recommended library of you love reading!

Amazing! Just no words to say. It was my first time to the Jurong regional library and the very scale of it took me in awe. So many collections of different books and so many different levels to go and explore! They have a community space for events and workshops , then there is a cafe and bakery inside with good food options. There is so many different things to explore! I couldn’t even finish going through all the sections! I will definitely go there again soon. The place is also located very close to the MRT  station and the Jurong east bus terminus . It has a sheltered walkway so we could easily reach the place even though it was raining. Many people from all different age groups were there. There is study tables with charging points. For some of the study tables you need to book in advance but for the rest you can always find and empty space. There are DVDs and other stuff as well. If you have to borrow books you need to register , but I just went for reading so I didn’t have to register anything. The library closes at 9pm and is open all days of the week. They will announce over the mike before the closing time so you have time to pack your stuff. I especially liked the photography section and farming section . There’s a separate magazine section and newspaper section as well. Different languages from Tamil to Malay to Korean you will find in the library .

This library was visited few times in the span of 15 years. Still impressed by its cleanliness, calm and  peaceful atmosphere in addition to the silent air, the display of new arrivals also catch my eyes. Always full of study-loaded students and other book-lovers, you will still find a seat for enjoying some books or surfing internet on the provided PC's as the library is very spacious. Remarkably, there is a vast collection of books.

Lakeside MRT Station is clean, well-maintained, and convenient for daily commuters. The station provides easy access to nearby residential areas, schools, and Jurong Lake Gardens. Trains are frequent, and the station is generally well organized.
The sheltered walkways and clear signage make traveling comfortable and convenient. A reliable MRT station for both residents and visitors.
🚆🇸🇬

I am reluctant to leave a negative review, but the service I received at the Passenger Service Centre (EZ-Link) was unacceptable. Both staff members on duty—a Chinese male and a Malay male—were dismissive and spoke in an aggressive, angry tone. Their half-hearted approach to assisting commuters is a poor reflection of the service line, and I hope management addresses this lack of professionalism. Negative star
Crowed MRT Station. I visited Jurong Lake Park via this station as Chinese garden was closed. There are some stores and food stall around the station, good point!
Great station with plenty of good food options to grab and go. Has a SAM machine, post box, POSB and OCBC ATMS.
Crowded during peak periods from 7-9am on weekdays and 5-7++pm on weekdays

I believe by now many are aware that the Nasi Lemak is no longer “power” like how they used to be back in the heydays of the early 2000s. However, they still serve a ‘slightly above average’ Nasi Lemak catering to both people prefer the standard basic options or those who want a side of various ingredients - I am in the camp of the former. As for the Otak Otak, thankfully it was a well spiced big flat piece that did not taste artificially coloured, unlike many Nasi Lemak stalls these days.

The sambal is not overly sweet that how most Nasi Lemak stalls have been unfortunately deviating to lately, sambal here as a balance of sweetness with savouriness and a tinge of spice.

Is it worth eating here? Yes if you’re in the vicinity but is it worth going out of the way for this Nasi Lemak - No, those days have been long gone.
Grabbed Set 1 + a potato cake at Power Nasi Lemak ($5.50). The taste was okay overall, but the chili was a bit too sweet for my liking. I would have preferred a more spicy sambal.
The $4.50 chicken set more than justified its price. It had everything you’d expect, but in larger portions. Nothing to complain about at this price point.

Closes at 3.30am so it's a good supper spot. Sambal chilli is really good. Fried chicken skin is crispy but meat is not marinated so lacks different layers of flavour. The rest of the items are okish. The price is reasonable and the service is fast.

1) The rice doesn't taste lemak. No go.
2) Chilli too sweet and gives salty after-taste. No go.
3) Chicken wing ok for me (crispy outside, moist inside, bone doesn't appear black). However my friend said his got the foul chicken smell. Maybe I'm lucky or too numb to it.
4) Egg is a bit rubbery (maybe pre-prepared for too long).
5) Price is average ($4.5 for chicken wing set)
6) Didn't get to try the ottah. (Sold out)
7) Queue is a killer. (1 star for atmosphere)

Personally, i wouldn't return for this nasi lemak. Residents living nearby probably ok/support this, but my take is traveling/queuing for this is a waste of time.

90 Boon Lay Place Food Court is one of the standout food centers in the area. It’s clean, well-maintained, and offers a fantastic variety of food. My personal go-tos are Er Jie Rojak, Old Street Scissors Cut Curry Rice, and the fried rice from the Zhi Char stall.
Separately, can really sense the high level of competition here, as quite a few stalls have closed recently and are currently waiting for new tenants to take over.

In all fairness, my fave stalls like the ErJie Rojak, cai peng, curry rice are good & I would give at least four stars.
Drinks are drinks, except for 10cts more or less for the hot drinks varies coffee shop by coffee shops.

What I do not agree, is charging 20cts for half a cup of hot water, to top up a cup of Chinese tea ordered earlier, that had cooled down.  If I had requested for a fresh cup of hot water, am happy to pay.
However, for the same cup & only requesting half a cup (as it’s half full), charging for this is daylight robbery.
I shd hv asked the neighbouring stall where we bought our food, and am sure she’ll be happy to top-up foc.

Some stalls still open either 24hrs or until last night. Arrived at around 12am. Dinner crowd is crazy packed.

Carpark charges in photo attached.

Great spot for gathering
Open air seating are wonderful
Very nice lighting and toilets are clean
Lots of parking spaces
Nearby bus stop
Great food choices
Speed cooking
Clean environment
Value for money
Ordered 2 noodles 1 rice. The noodles so salty as if salt is free and totally not like the photos shown. Taste so bad! Waited for very long as there seems like no system. Waste money only.

Suzuki Coffee is one of those hidden gems that proves great coffee lovers are willing to travel. Tucked away in an industrial area and not particularly easy to reach by public transport, I was surprised to find the café quite crowded on a weekend morning. That alone says a lot about its reputation

The exterior is striking, with large coffee-themed murals covering the roastery buildings and a spacious industrial setting that gives the place plenty of character. Inside, the atmosphere is calm, minimalist, and distinctly Japanese-inspired. Warm wood furnishings, clean lines, and soft lighting create a peaceful environment that feels far removed from the bustle of the city

Despite the crowd, the space never felt overly noisy. Many visitors were quietly enjoying their coffee, working on laptops, reading, or chatting with friends. The large window-side seating provides plenty of natural light and makes for a relaxing place to spend an hour or two

The coffee menu offers a good selection of beans with detailed tasting notes, ranging from chocolatey and nutty profiles to more floral and fruity options. I tried an iced flat white, which was smooth, well-balanced, and refreshing without being overly bitter. It is clear that coffee is taken seriously here

You’d love this cafe spot tucked in an industrial area ☺️
A Japan-style inspired interior, cozy, quiet, delicious coffee. Travel down here for a little getaway with a decent cup of coffee and enjoy it with a book in hand.. perfect spot for you to escape the SG crowds.

They have a few coffee bean selections to choose from, save your mind and let the friendly baristas to recommend for you ☺️

Visited one of Singapore's oldest coffee roasteries which is located in an industrial area. A quiet traditional Japanese tea house with nice zen-feel atmosphere

Matcha latte ~ smooth, creamy and earthy
¿ Iced Cascara Lemonade ~ perfect balance" of fruity and refreshing

Chocoalte brownie ~ satisfying delicious
Sanjuen Dorayaki Matcha ~ light, moist texture with matcha bitterness sweetness

Very pleasant place! Double story with plenty of choices for beans. Except that there is limited choice of food and not cheap for a coffee - about 8.5 nett. But definitely worth a try in the west given very limited cafes around here.

Very cozy nice little cafe at West! This will be my favorite spot for coffee at west area. And westerner deserve more good coffee cafe like this.

They have babycino for kids also.

place_name: Bok's Kitchen by Hidden Chefs - Singapore
review_text:
It was our first time dining at Bok’s Kitchen, and overall we had a nice experience. We ordered freshly brewed iced lemon tea, truffle carbonara, chilli crab pasta, and crispy pork cheek.

The crispy pork cheek was fantastic, especially when eaten hot, as it tends to lose some of its texture once it cools. The chilli crab pasta had quite a strong level of spiciness, so those who are less tolerant of spicy food may want to take note (not sure if a less spicy option is available). The truffle carbonara was enjoyable, though my husband would have preferred a slightly thicker sauce.

Overall, it was a pleasant dining experience, and we enjoyed trying the dishes

place_name: Bok's Kitchen by Hidden Chefs - Singapore
review_text:
The crispy pork cheek was love at first bite—super crunchy outside, tender inside, and the sweet Thai chilli sauce tied it all together. Portion? Generous.

I went for the grilled chicken burger with sweet potato fries (love that upgrade option ✨). The patty was juicy, though flavour-wise pretty average. Luckily, the sauces made it a messy, sauce-lover’s kind of burger.

Since they accept CDC vouchers, it does get pretty packed and noisy, but hey—good vibes if you’re into the bustle. Overall: not gourmet, but hearty, affordable, and satisfying.

Would Deinfitely come back to abuse the cdc voucher again

place_name: Bok's Kitchen by Hidden Chefs - Singapore
review_text:
This place is a new gem! Definitely deserves our 5-star review.

The char siew rice is a must try! The mushroom soup comes with two pieces of toasted garlic bread. Probably one of the most garlicky breads I have ever had. If you are not a fan of garlic, you might want to skip it, but I personally loved it!

Will definitely be back to try more items on the menu. 😊

place_name: Bok's Kitchen by Hidden Chefs - Singapore
review_text: I completely agree — Bok’s Kitchen is a hidden gem that’s well worth the return trip! The atmosphere is incredibly cozy and welcoming, making you feel right at home. On top of that, the food is delicious and shiok. I’ll definitely be coming back for more.

place_name: Bok's Kitchen by Hidden Chefs - Singapore
review_text:
Visited Bok's Kitchen upon a recommendation by a colleague. If you are driving, it might be a bit hard to spot the place. It's located on the extreme left hand side of the building (when you are facing the building).

For the main dishes, we ordered:
Spinach Mushroom Aglio
Truffle Carbonara
Char Siew Rice Bowl
Salmon Teriyaki Rice Bowl

We prefer the pasta dishes over the rice bowl ones. Portion is considered small but the quality of food is definitely good. Question of quality over quantity 😊

For the side dishes, we ordered:
Bok's Garlic Winglet
Crispy Pork Cheek

Both side dishes are worth trying. Crispy and tasty.

We wanted to try the chili crab pasta that many customers are raving about but it wasn't available during lunch.

We will be back to try the other dishes in the menu. Recommended place to visit if you are in the vicinity.

Neat and clean mini mart that sells convenient kitchen essentials like fresh loose vegetables, eggs, noodles, etc. Amongst other things. There's quite a range of halal frozen goods! Accepts PayNow and a few other cashless payments too.
2min walk from the bus stop and I'm glad to live nearby.

This mart is the best!! They sell he best items at best prices like my favourite durian potong for 80cents and jasmine green tea for 1.20 dollars. The people working at the mart are very kind and friendly! Thank you uncle!!
A nice and well organised minimart. Daily groceries are in reasonable price. Staffs are friendly and helpful. Happy to have this neighbourhood minimart
The Mart is well-organized and well-lit with plenty of room to move around. Also, they offer a selection of frozen food products for sale.
great place to buy everyday item, the store keeper also nice to serve customer, and the price is good. another recommended place to visit. thanx
The all item very fresh in the market

Probably the last vestiges of a generation lost in memory, this market is wet alright. No frills. No thrills. Just stuffs that only Merdeka and Pioneer generation aunties will buy.

That's only if you cook and don't have a helper at home. Decidedly utilitarian thus is how a market was in the 60s

Hawker food. An indian stall selling tosei, appom and variety of food. There is a corner malay stall selling lots of kuih2, mee siam, also prata and some others. A few stall not open yet. These 2 stall open as early as 5.30am.
Nostalgic experience,old vibes nice food.if you are a mee goteng fanatic this is a place to go.variety of malay foods amd chinese foods.highly recomend
Went there for breakfast. How food prices has gone up after the year end clean up of the market and food centre from my last visit last year.

New Revamped Jurong East Swimming Complex with Lazy and waterplay areas for the kids. Hv a good wave generation pool which operates every hour.A bit sad that the water slides has been replaced with a new children water play area for toddlers and young children. Not enough shaded canopies for users of the facility to keep their belongings the heat on hot sunny days. No food vending machines for snacks, only hv a drinks vending machine.
Fun time with kiddos. The toilets are great with large cubicles and compartments to hang your dry clothes. Definitely a comeback for my family.

Although I must say that I missed the huge slides that my generation grew up with. But it is a nice transitional change to me being a Dad now, bringing my little kiddos in what is now a kids play area.

Also the toilets have had a face-lift. Kids changing room is nice and clean. Adults changing room is equally good.

They had a baby pool, kids playground, kids splash area, lazy river and a wave pool. Wave pool had waves every hour lasting about 15mins each time. Lazy river is where we spent most of our time at, easy going and fun. Rates are affordable with activesg. Minus one star because I'd prefer having the slides.

Went with family on a Saturday. Place was busy but still have space for a good swim. Kids enjoyed the new water play area and wave pool (though wave was mild and very far apart). The lazy river is still there.

A newly opened playground in the park with clean facilities and a nice play area for kids
There is a toilet nearby, which is very convenient for everyone especially families with young children.

Do note it can get quite hot around noon as there’s limited shade. There isn’t a vending machine so it’s good to bring own drinks and snacks.

Overall, good addition to the park and great place for kids to spend their energy!
Nice new park. But partially open only. Cant wait to see what else is there. There’s another area with no playground where not much people!
Nice park with a sheltered playground for kids. Good to go in the morning. There are water coolers around and a toilet just beside the playground.

This playground is surprisingly impressive, offering an extensive variety of play items that keep children of all ages engaged with its diverse climbing structures and interactive stations. It’s a fantastic spot for a family afternoon, though it is important to note that there is currently no dedicated on-site parking, so you will need to utilize a nearby HDB carpark instead.
Looks nice, hope it can last longer than the equipment at Jurong Park. And please don't park at Street 22, pity the bus drivers can't make the turn.

Sungei Buloh Wetland Reserve is one of the best places in Singapore to experience nature in its most authentic form. From the moment I entered via the Neo Tiew Crescent entrance, I was surrounded by lush greenery, peaceful trails, and the soothing sounds of wildlife

The reserve is exceptionally well-maintained, with clear signage, wide boardwalks, and excellent visitor facilities at the Wetland Centre. The elevated wooden walkways make it easy to explore the mangrove forests while enjoying beautiful views of the surrounding wetlands

One of the highlights of my visit was observing mudskippers hopping across the mudflats among the mangrove roots. It was fascinating to see these unique creatures thriving in their natural habitat. The reserve offers plenty of opportunities to spot wildlife, so keep an eye out for monitor lizards, crabs, and migratory birds

I was also impressed by the Mudskipper Pod, a beautifully designed wooden observation structure that blends seamlessly into the environment. It provides a cool and shaded resting point with panoramic views over the mangroves and the Johor Strait. The architecture adds a unique touch to the overall experience without detracting from the natural surroundings

A refreshing escape into nature! We enjoyed a slow and peaceful walk at Sungei Buloh Wetland Reserve on May 27, fully immersed in the calm beauty of the wetlands. The walking paths were beautifully lined with lush trees, creating a naturally shaded and cooling environment that made the stroll especially comfortable even during the warmer hours.
The tranquil surroundings, gentle sounds of nature, and fresh air provided a relaxing break from the busy city atmosphere. Along the way, we appreciated the well-maintained trails and the opportunity to observe the rich biodiversity and greenery up close.
It is a wonderful place for a leisurely walk, nature appreciation, photography, or simply to unwind and recharge. Highly recommended for anyone looking to enjoy a peaceful and scenic outdoor experience in Singapore.

A beautiful and peaceful escape into nature! The mangroves, fresh air, and rich wildlife make every walk feel special. Perfect for a relaxing stroll or bird watching—especially in the early morning or evening.
Stay curious and keep your eyes open… you might spot a giant monitor lizard crossing the path or even a crocodile resting in the water. A little adventure awaits you here!

My family took a late afternoon walk down to Sungei Buloh during low tide today to unwind, and it was a great way to clear our heads. The air had that familiar, heavy scent of salt and damp mangrove mud, and with the water stripped back, the exposed api-api roots and glistening mudflats completely changed the landscape. We spent a while just watching the giant mudskippers drag themselves across the silt and bicker over territory, which is always entertaining. The low water also meant the local birds were out in full force. We managed to spot a Great Egret freezing in place before spearing a small fish, and a Grey Heron standing completely still in the shallows waiting for Archerfish. Finishing up on the main bridge during the golden hour was the highlight, looking down into the shallow river channel at schools of Halfbeaks skimming the surface under the evening sky was incredibly peaceful and a solid reminder of how nice it is to have this kind of wilderness right on our doorstep.
Really nice nature walk. There is one bus that goes there. Can spend about 3-4 hours there. You get to see the swamps, facing JB, lizards, fishes, crabs, birds etc.

A lovely small rainforest park tucked away in the west that feels surprisingly immersive despite its compact size. The walking trails are well-paved and shaded by tall trees, making it comfortable for both casual strolls and light workouts. There’s also a playground and a simple fitness corner, so it works well for families as well as those looking for a quick exercise session

One of the highlights is the lookout point overlooking the quarry — a peaceful spot where you can pause, enjoy the greenery, and listen to the sounds of nature. Wildlife sightings are quite common too; it’s not unusual to spot colourful birds, squirrels, and even monkeys if you’re lucky. History buffs will appreciate the remnants of WWII structures such as old pillboxes scattered around the park, which add a subtle layer of heritage to the natural scenery

The only downside at the moment is that the path leading to the Syonan Chureito (the WWII Japanese remembrance site) is under maintenance, so access is temporarily closed

It was my first and hope it's not the last visit to this place. I really want to go there again.

Really liked the place and enjoyed walking around this place. This would definitely be a cute for all of your sadness.

Didn't realise there was a quarry in the park, took a long walk in nature and found many wonderful things! Was caught by a sudden downpour, appreciated the thick trees around the area which sheltered me from the rain.

Good mix of trails, some paths are cobblestones so be in a proper footwear.

Caught a squirrel in action too!

🌳A lush greenery that is just a stone throw away from the Bukit Batok Housing Estate, providing residents living here a green lung to relax, exercise and be with nature.
🚶‍➡️🏃‍➡️Many came here in the morning for exercises and brisk walk.
🛝🏋️At the Bt Batok Nature Park, besides having numerous shelters and walking paths, there is a Children Playground for little ones to enjoy, a Foot Reflexology Path and an Adult Fitness Corner that are next to the Playground.
🏞️But the centrepiece is the Lookout Point, where one can rest, take in the sight of the tranquil water and it's scenic surrounding.
⛩️Another interesting place around the Park with World War II history is Syonan Chureito Memorial
🚧Unfortunately, the access path from the park proper is closed for safety reason and is undergoing stabilisation work which is expected to be completed end Dec 2027.
Nevertheless, the site can be reached from Lorong Sesuai.
🪨Fun fact:
Bukit Batok Nature Park was formerly a granite quarry site.
Like many other granite quarry around, it ceased operation for safety and environmental reasons. And was then transformed into a nature park for all to enjoy.
Bukit batok natural park nice and peaceful park see sunrise depend on the cloud good place exercise 2 jan 2026
Just keep cycling... You will reach your destination .. ✊

Peaceful & breezy. One of the historical places.

‘The Battle of Sarimbun Beach was the first stage of the Japanese assault on Singapore in February 1942 during World War II.’ - Wikipedia
I hiked all the way in, and it was peaceful and quiet.
Very good place to learn history from the boards.

Kallang Park Connector is an absolute gem for anyone who appreciates a bit of wilderness in the heart of the city. As a regular spot for jogging, it offers much more than just a paved path; the air is incredibly fresh, making those morning or evening runs feel revitalizing. What really sets this route apart, though, is the incredible biodiversity. During my visits, I’ve encountered everything from vibrant insects to the occasional snakes in the river—nature is definitely thriving here.

For those who find themselves constantly looking toward the water, the sightings of various fish species are a fantastic bonus that adds to the charm of the area. It’s the perfect blend of a functional fitness route and a mini-safari. Whether you're working on your cardio or just looking to clear your head, the connection to nature here is top-notch. If you're looking for an all-in-one spot to stay active and enjoy the outdoors, this is the place to be. Highly recommended for nature enthusiasts and fitness lovers alike!

Kallang Park Connector is a linear green trail in Singapore’s Park Connector Network (PCN) that runs along the Kallang River and links Bishan-Ang Mo Kio Park in the north to Kallang Riverside Park in the south.

It’s about 7.87 km long and provides a scenic route for walking, jogging, and cycling through several central residential areas

We definitely enjoyed our walk along the Kallang PCN.

There's a weird sign pointing towards bishan park (the wrong way), confused us for a moment.

One of the most scenic park connector in Singapore linking Clark Quay and City center to Kallang, Kallang Bahru, Potong Pasir and all the way to Bishan Park. Perfect for cycling, jogging and brisk walking.
Very good area for relax your mind

The 2 stretches of Kallang Park connector that go under the bridges are finally open this week! Lovely artwork. Kudos to the artists that put them up and pat on the back for the workers whp were involved in the construction.  Joggers and cyclists can now bypass 2 traffic lights at Kallang Bahru and Upper Boon Keng Road, to get from Bendemeer Road to Kallang MRT. Celebration!

place_name: Raffles Kidz @ Jurong West (Yuan Ching) - Best Childcare and Preschool Singapore
review_text: Our child transferred from China to K2 at Raffles Kidz Pres. Thanks to all teachers’ patient care, especially Chinese teacher Wen, he adapted fast and enjoys school life now. We are satisfied with the school’s warm environment and teaching.

place_name: Raffles Kidz @ Jurong West (Yuan Ching) - Best Childcare and Preschool Singapore
review_text:
Our son has been in school for 1.5yrs since he was 18mo. During transition from PN to Nursery was when we were given feedback on his development, and at which point we considered changing schools for something that was more suitable for him. Principal Gwen stepped in and offered a change in class and teachers and we agreed to see if this allowed our son to enjoy school and learn better. We are so glad we were encouraged to this step because fast forward 6m later, our son has shown so much growth in social, emotional, physical & cognitive development. We must especially commend Teacher Arinah and Xing Lao Shi, his form teachers, to whom both have been very dedicated in caring and guiding our son along the way. Particularly, there was more feedback provided to us either by phone calls or after school drop off chats — that helped us to also support the teachers work in helping our son’s progress. Our son has since been much happier in going and talking about school.

We would also like to thank Teacher Adillah, Zann and Anna who also made school so much more enjoyable with their warmth and investment to really engaging (chatting) with our boy!

place_name: Raffles Kidz @ Jurong West (Yuan Ching) - Best Childcare and Preschool Singapore
review_text: The teachers are always so welcoming during drop-off and pick-up which gives parents great peace of mind. We transferred our son here and it has definitely been the right choice. The teachers have been patient with him especially during meal times and we’ve seen great progress that he is now toilet trained. Lessons are also well rounded and engaging. Shoutout to Teacher Zann and Xing 老师 for their dedication and care.

place_name: Raffles Kidz @ Jurong West (Yuan Ching) - Best Childcare and Preschool Singapore
review_text:
Raffles Kidz has been an amazing preschool experience for our daughter. From the very first day, the teachers made her feel incredibly warm, welcomed, and safe. You can really feel their dedication — they take the time to understand each child’s personality, and they create an environment where the kids are excited to learn and explore.
Our daughter looks forward to school every morning, and we’ve seen her grow so much in confidence, language, and social skills. The curriculum is engaging, the activities are thoughtfully planned, and the entire team communicates well with parents.
We’re truly grateful to the teachers and staff at Raffles Kidz for giving our little one such a happy and nurturing start to her learning journey. Highly recommended for any parent looking for a caring and enriching preschool!

place_name: Raffles Kidz @ Jurong West (Yuan Ching) - Best Childcare and Preschool Singapore
review_text:
My kids have attended Raffles Kidz since Pre-Nursery and I can say with confidence, “No regrets at all!”

The curriculum is enriching and holistic with plenty of engaging activities that supports both academic and social development. Stars of the centre have to be the teachers and principal! Special mention to Tr Triff, 牛老师, Tr Rosz and 张老师!

Tr Triff and Niu Laoshi have played a significant role in my boy’s early education journey, building a strong foundation for my son as he graduates from K2 this year. I’m truly grateful for their commitment in nurturing my boy, helping him reach his full potential and preparing him well for primary school.

I also deeply appreciate Zhang Laoshi and Tr Rosz for their unwavering dedication and effort towards nurturing my daughter who is currently in N2. With their warmth, patience and guidance, my girl is able to learn and grow in a positive environment, and she has showed tremendous improvement in all aspects.

I definitely highly recommend RKI! For parents looking for a good pre-school, Raffles Kidz is your top choice.

I am writing to express my profound appreciation and heartfelt commendation for one of your exceptional team members, Ah Long, whose outstanding service on Friday, 30 January 2026, left an indelible impression on our entire organization.

On that afternoon, our team visited your Jurong Superbowl outlet to purchase supplies for our upcoming community outreach event—8 cartons of mandarin oranges and 2 cartons of mineral water. What began as a routine shopping trip transformed into an extraordinary demonstration of customer service excellence that deserves the highest recognition.

Ah Long's dedication went far beyond the call of duty. With meticulous care and genuine concern for quality, he personally opened and inspected every single carton of mandarin oranges, ensuring that each piece met the highest standards. When he discovered items that fell short, he promptly arranged replacements without hesitation. This level of attention to detail speaks volumes about his commitment to customer satisfaction.

But his exceptional service did not stop there. Understanding the weight and volume of our purchases, Ah Long took the initiative to load everything onto a push cart. Then, in an act of kindness that truly moved us, he personally pushed the cart across the road to our office building and helped us transport everything up to the 3rd floor—all with a cheerful and willing spirit.

Perhaps what impressed us most was Ah Long's unwavering integrity. When my colleague attempted to offer him a monetary tip in appreciation for his extraordinary efforts, he politely but firmly declined. He even refused a simple drink of water, respectfully explaining that Sheng Siong's service policy guides his actions. This display of professionalism and principle is genuinely rare and deeply admirable.

Ah Long embodies the very best of what customer service should be: attentive, proactive, generous, and principled. His exceptional magnanimity and genuine desire to serve reflect not only his personal character but also the outstanding training and values that Sheng Siong instills in its team members.

Thank you for cultivating such remarkable talent. Your commitment to service excellence through team members like Ah Long sets a gold standard for the retail industry.

That said, it's an experience you should try at least once. We scored 5 huge tiger prawns for just $40, which is incredible value considering they'd likely cost $20–$30 per piece in a restaurant. You'll see many traditional fish vendors selling their fresh catch straight from the ships.

Just be mindful that the primary audience here are fish mongers supplying traditional wet market stalls, so navigate accordingly.

Note: bring your Singpass, to get through security.

Jurong West Swimming Complex — Hidden Gem Alert

Best Sunday experience, hands down. Not crazy crowded, which is a rare win in Singapore. The lap pool is bliss—can actually swim freely without dodging elbows. Bonus: it’s shaded from the sun, so no need to fry while doing laps.

Even under CAT1 lightning alerts, you can still swim (huge plus). The kids’ pool is a comfortable 0.9m depth, perfect for little ones. And yes—warm showers in the children’s facilities. Game changer.

There’s a decent water park setup for kids and a surprisingly long water slide that’s way more fun than expected. Honestly, this place is the most underrated swimming complex in Singapore. Total hidden gem. Don’t sleep on it.

A multi-story complex consisting of a stadium, swimming pool with water feature like slides and lazy river. The. Complex also houses Gymnasium, child care, tuition center, dance hall, cafe.....etc.
A complex cater to all age group. You can literally spent a whole day here. Car park is at complex ground level so no worries even if it rain.
As of 23 April 2022, the children play pool is still closed due to some maintenance works

Came here for the swimming lessons. The water playground is closed until further notice. ActiveSG staff is very friendly and helpful. Common areas are very clean. Floor of Toilet nearest to pool is quite dirty. Likely due to the extra human traffic with wet swimming attire, and not the fault of the cleaners.

Update (01Jun2022) - Water playground is partially opened. The mega slide is still closed. Lots of kids are playing in the water playground.

Free entry for members. Free for my 2yr old but need to pay $1.75 for my 7 yr old. There 4 swim areas. 3 for kids and 1 lap pool. Very spacious, change and bathing areas are conducive. Not very very fun but good enough for 1-2hrs of play time for kids.

There’s many eateries here by the way! Jacks place, indo food & Prata!

With water guns, fountains, controllable pouring rain and a nice depth of 0.8m, it's good fun for the kids. The nice touch is that the frog pool is heated so no fears of morning cold swims! Also, the showers are heated too! Would come again!

A beautiful, relaxing and vibrant place for walks, for taking a step back from the hectic pace.
Spending 3 hours walking and appreciating the fresh air, the manicured gardens, the lake and wonderful architecture, helped to destress and reset.
If you've not been, well worth some time out.

Definitely one of the nicest and must go to parks in Singapore if you are alone, couples, families group of friends wanting to escape from the concrete jungle of the city. Jurong used to be perceived as a very faraway venue to commute to but with so many new MRT train lines it’s so much easier and faster to reach. Do take note of opening hours. Free entry and easily accessible with many entrances by Chinese Garden MRT or Lakeside MRT and buses. Great for all activities, walking , strolling, jogging, cycling, picnics and there is even a skate park and dog run. What makes this park stand out from the rest is the sheer size, the large lake with canoeing; stunning replicas of historical Chinese and Japanese architecture and pagodas, bonsai garden and wildlife. And especially the stunning Lily Flower Pond at the Japanese Garden and the Lotus Flower Pond at the Chinese Garden. Definitely worth to make a trip down to enjoy this green oasis escapade rich in flora and fauna and water. It’s very tranquil and peaceful from Mondays to Saturdays. Ample toilets and drinking water dispenser and also a beverage shop selling nice cold beverages.

NParks really did a good job in revamping this park.
There's plenty of interesting plants and wildlife to spot.
Took me and my friends 3 hours to casually explore the entire area.
It's definitely a nice place to run in the mornings/evenings.

I highly recommend to visit this gardens, as there are a lot of attractions to see and they are all free! Mr Gao is a very kind-hearted and, more importantly, a passionate teacher. Coming from a non-Chinese-speaking family, I have seen him consistently go the extra mile to organise study materials and learning activities that keep my son proactively engaged, while thoughtfully taking into account his abilities and potential.

Mr Gao has a strong ability to read the child and understands how the curriculum should be adapted to meet individual learning needs. Not every teacher has the patience or time to provide such a personalised learning experience, and we are truly grateful for the positive impact he has had on our son. As a result, he is beginning to genuinely enjoy learning Chinese.

Mr Chin offers genuine and wholesome lessons. Having booked multiple 1 on 1 lessons with him, I experienced a teacher who would go above and beyond in order to secure the future his students deserve first handedly. He has taught me extremely well and definitely influenced the way I perceive the world around me.

- Malcolm Heng
I used to just barely pass my physics in secondary 3, but with mr chin’s help and encouragement, i managed to improve my physics grade by 2, from C6 straight to B4.
Very much

I’ve been a regular customer at the drink stall and generally enjoy the drinks, but I wanted to share some feedback about the service.

I’ve noticed a difference in how customers are treated. When I order (as a non-Chinese customer), the interaction often feels less polite, for example, the tone when taking orders and the way the drink is handed over. In contrast, Chinese customers seem to receive a friendlier and more attentive service.

The difference is noticeable enough to affect the experience. As a regular customer, I hope the service can be more consistent and welcoming to everyone, regardless of background.

The food here is generally expensive, especially at the mixed-rice stall where staff are often unsure of dish prices and attitude not good, and the displayed price list appears unreliable. Only the chicken rice is reasonably priced, while the duck rice is delicious and worth trying. The Chinese food stall used to offer generous portions that justified the price, but now the portions have been noticeably reduced — it feels very disappointing

Very disappointing experience at the drink stall in Innovation Groove Food Court. I ordered an ice lemon tea, but the staff responded with a confused “huh” and seemed unable to understand basic English. Communication was difficult and unprofessional, and I had to repeat my order through a coworker.
The drink was eventually made, but the overall service felt rude and dismissive. Customer service in an F&B setting should be polite and respectful, regardless of language ability.
Hope management can look into staff training and improve service standards.
Love the China food. It's a flat rate for 1rice with 2 dish!. The taste is superb. Great fix for lunch during office hr.
Food price at industrial area , consider abit high .  Roasted chicken price still consider ok .

It will be great if they can deep fried the chicken to keep the crispness and juiciness

The rice is very fragrant. Drumstick is very skinny. Likely a diet chicken.  Chilli is nice.

I really don't understand why so many people are giving it good reviews. I've been to this store many times and haven't found anything special about it. Last time I bought two packs, and my wife and I both had diarrhea for two days. I think it must be something wrong with the chicken; the taste is still weird, just like before.
Never going to support this shop again! Very disappointing!. We are paying for the food and the quantity packed is so little!. Don't treat your customers like this!. 👎👎👎
$5 to or the Ayam Berempah set! Very economical

One of the worst kueh I've eaten in my years of living... Again n again being disappointed with their kueh quality.. but I chose to give chance.. today is just the worst. ondeh2 was 90¢ each . It has no aroma , the filling is not gula Malacca at all.. not forgetting the glutinous ball is so bland... Just tasteless...Feels so cheated !
Thai products minimart.

Decided to go to BFT Taman Jurong for a trial because I was contemplating on switching gyms for convenience sake with regards to distance from home and my work schedule.

The coaches and community are welcoming and I felt comfortable during my sessions there! 🙌🏽 Different workouts everyday to focus on different muscle group and cardio/strength so you’ll never be bored working out here ;)

Became a member after my last day of trial and the admin who attended to me was so kind, helpful and patient in answering my queries etc! Thank You so much for your kindness, prompt service recovery and swift replies! 🌻

Looking forward to making this gym my second home now 😆

I’d give this place 10 🌟 if i could!!!

Even after a long day at work, I still look forward to coming to the gym because I somehow always leave feeling more energized afterwards. The atmosphere, the people and the energy here just hit different.

The studio manager, head coach and coaches really put in their 100% for the members. They’ve created such a welcoming and inclusive environment where people of all fitness levels can feel comfortable training at their own pace while still feeling encouraged and supported. You never feel judged or out of place here.

You can really feel the effort they put into building the community beyond workouts too. From getting to know members personally to organising activities and creating such a positive culture, it feels more than just a gym. It’s the kind of place that makes people genuinely enjoy showing up and keeps you wanting to come back! 💙

Really glad I signed up for this! It’s the first place that makes exercising such a sustainable routine for me and I think it’s largely due to the fun and interesting workouts and also the very friendly and professional coaches who will always push you to go above and beyond! I have attended the sessions consistently for a month and I feel my fitness level improving and I’m becoming abit stronger than before! Kudos to the team and continue to keep up the great work!

Side note that facilities are also kept clean which is a plus! But would be good to include more dumbbells at the lighter range (between 4 to 6kg) as there may not be enough when classes are full.

Great experience here! The environment is welcoming and motivating, which makes it easier to stay consistent. The coaches are friendly and attentive—they take the time to guide you and ensure proper form. The classes are also beginner-friendly and well-structured, and the community is supportive and kind, which makes a big difference. Also, the coaches always have really good playlists, which makes the workouts even more enjoyable!

Ive been training at BFT Taman Jurong and it has been a really great experience so far. The gym environment is welcoming and motivating, which makes it easy to stay consistent.
Special thanks to coaches Raine, Danial, Chris, Kesavan, Daniel, Jeethin, Grace, Phoebe, and Eijah. They are always encouraging during workouts and very attentive in correcting my form. I have found a safe space where I can work towards my fitness goals! 💪🏻

Supposely to be a "NO SMOKING" zone at this park. But we came across smokers (especially students and teenagers) smoking around the Futsal and BBQ area.

Nearby NPP police should patrol these areas more often as a deterrent and to enforce the "NO SMOKING" regulations in the park.
Taman Jurong Greens is located in Taman Jurong and is one of Singapore's oldest residential areas. Now, other than HD.
My kids love to play the playground. And for those who love jogging that is a best place if u have a kids

This is a very good park with running, jogging tracks and multi sports facilities. It is also equipped with gym equipment for workouts. These day's this park is getting popular and attracting neighbourhood crowd.

The newly renovated park with new amenities make this place much more synergy together with the residents here. With new basketball courts with shelter, new playground for kids, exercise area for all.. Make us whole...

If you’re looking for a peaceful retreat that feels miles away from the city without actually leaving it, Rasau Walk at Jurong Lake Gardens is an absolute gem. I recently spent several minutes here, and it is easily one of the most picturesque boardwalks in Singapore

Rasau Walk, located in Jurong Lake Gardens, is a peaceful boardwalk surrounded by lush greenery and wetlands. Named after the Rasau tree, it offers scenic views of aquatic plants like lotus flowers and water lilies. The family-friendly trail is perfect for birdwatching and spotting dragonflies. It provides a tranquil escape, ideal for a leisurely stroll or relaxation.

The best part of Jurong Lake Gardens. The definite visit time would be at night when all the surrounding is dark, where you see river of orange light along the pathway. Very nice. There are several public benches but no rain shelter, and it's totally understandable as you don't want to break the view.
Good view, but not as the photo shown on maps, the wood is old, but it’s still a great walk and really enjoyable.

Rasau Walk is a scenic Boardwalk that meanders along the freshwater Jurong Lake's water edge. This carefully restored natural habitat is home to a variety of Palm Trees that include Nibong, Sealing Wax (aka Lipstick Palm) and Rasau (Screwpine) aka Mengkuang Laut in Malay.
Others include
-Nipah Palm, - "attap chee", attap roof thatches
-Salak Palm - Salak or snake fruit
-Sago Palm - sago
The habitat is also a feeding ground for shore birds.
Come enjoy the tranquility of the Rasau Walk snaking along the water's edge and try spotting the attap seeds and salak fruits.

As a nearby resident, I visit Jurong Lake Gardens quite frequently. The walking tracks are always well-maintained, and I enjoy the scenic lake views. There are also plenty of shaded paths, which make for a very relaxing walk.

A pleasant but fairly small section of Jurong Lake Gardens. The landscaping is neat and well maintained, and there are some nice plants and boardwalk paths to stroll through, but it doesn’t take very long to cover the entire area

It’s fine to visit if you’re already nearby or doing a full loop of the gardens, but probably not worth going out of your way just for this section alone

The newly renovated Jurong Park @Chinese Garden @Japanese Garden is amazing. Many interesting plants, ponds, and fixtures are unique to only this park. Many themes are nicely decorated.

Food stores are scattered everywhere while facilities like toilets and water dispensers are generously available.

It's a good park for exploration, evening walks, and slow biking.

A huge garden! Took about 2 hours to slowly stroll, enjoy the view and reach the other end of the garden. It’s home to many animals and there’s so much to see. My favorite starts from the Japanese Garden onwards. Really enjoy the peaceful environment there and nice place to totally unwind. It’s a short walking distance from the MRT station.
nice and peaceful place. great for walks, jogs and runs. some do cycle around.

It's convenient where it is right by the bus and train stop. It ensures security with the keycard systems, and has a very cool garden themed pool area. The fact it has it's own pick up and drop off area is good, with a nice tennis court. The place has decent views.

Some improvements needed for the turnstile gate as the face ID can sometimes detect faces from afar, causing a bit of a hassle to get through the gate sometimes. Staying here isn't cheap either.

Very near MRT, very good place to go to, it is just that there are way too many mosquitoes (at the side facing the canal) and even monkeys went to the Balcony! Overall experience was Good, and even some inca doves went to nest in the balcony!

I wish to lodge a complaint against one of your Lakefront Residences security guards, Ainah.

I am a frequent visitor to this condominium and on 1 September at 1725, I did not register my visit with the guard house as I was in a hurry and a resident was just about to exit the premises. I waited at the side gate to enter when your security guard Ainah, started shouting at me from across the road in an extremely rude and condescending manner.

"Sir, you've been here many times before right? You know you need to register right? You not resident, you think free free can enter ah?"

After I had provided my details, she looked towards the side gate scornfully as if to say: "Get lost and now you may enter."

For the record, this was the first time that I did not register my visit and while I do not dispute the need to register, the manner and tone in which your staff reacted was utterly appalling, unnecessary and unwarranted.

As I was in a hurry, I did not want to waste any more time with her so I decided to let the matter rest then. After my visit, I did go back to the guardhouse and fortunately for her, she was no longer there otherwise I certainly would have given her a piece of my mind as I think her abhorrent behaviour has no place in our society.

I would like to believe that she is but one of the few black sheep in your organisation, and as a service organisation, I think you would agree with me that good service is of utmost importance. I therefore hope your organisation will take the appropriate action against this errant security staff so that the residents and visitors to Lakefront Residences will no longer have to put up with staff like her.
We love it here unit is a bit small but then again most units in Singapore are
mic check started from 9 am, playing super noisy music from 9:20. Strongly suggest people who need no sleep live here hahaha!
